The C++ Alliance
Empowering C++
Mission
Mission
Empowering the C++ Community
Our Mission
We make C++ accessible, safe, and powerful for everyone from students discovering computer architecture to engineers building the critical infrastructure our world depends on.

The Challenges
C++ powers our modern world, and the ecosystem faces critical challenges that demand action.

Resources and sustainability. World-class libraries maintained by passionate volunteers deserve proper support. Educational materials remain fragmented and inaccessible. Transformative C++ programs go unbuilt because talented developers lack the resources to bring their visions to life.

Safety and security. Memory vulnerabilities threaten critical infrastructure, from hospitals to power grids to financial systems. The language that runs our world must evolve to meet modern security demands without sacrificing its legendary performance.

Access and opportunity. C++ is an efficient, general-purpose language that teaches fundamental principles and opens global career opportunities. Yet barriers to entry prevent countless aspiring developers from discovering its power.

Our Solutions
We fund excellence. Supporting the maintenance and evolution of peer-reviewed libraries like Boost. Advancing compiler development and tooling that brings new language features to developers faster. Building essential tools like MrDocs for documentation and advancing proposals for memory safety features that prevent errors at compile time.

We build community. Administering the world's largest C++ Slack workspace where enthusiasts, standards committee members, and library developers collaborate globally. Sponsoring education at CppCon and conferences worldwide.

We shape the future. Motivating contributions to the C++ international standard through proposals and defect reports. When the language needs to evolve, we provide the resources to make it happen.

Why It Matters
C++ quietly powers humanity's greatest technological achievements and most critical systems. Our work ensures it doesn't just endure—it thrives: safer, more accessible, and ready for decades of innovation ahead.

Every developer deserves world-class tools and education. Every critical system deserves secure code. Every brilliant idea deserves the resources to become reality.

This is what we're building.

FAQ
FAQ
Given the existence of Standard C++ Foundation and Software Freedom Conservancy, why was it necessary to form The C++ Alliance as a new, separate legal entity?
While the missions of Standard C++ Foundation and The C++ Alliance are similar and overlapping, they have different status under tax law. The Foundation is organized as a 501(c)(6) organization, a trade group. In addition to providing legal protection for companies to work together in ways that might otherwise be viewed as industrial "collusion," a trade group can accept tax-deductible dues from member companies. The Alliance is organized as a 501(c)(3) organization, a charity which can accept tax deductible contributions from the general public.

Although the tax status of Software Freedom Conservancy and The C++ Alliance are similar, their missions and concerns are different. There is mission overlap. Conservancy, whose mission is to support the development of FLOSS libraries, supports the Boost Libraries project, which would also be within the mission of The Alliance, but The Alliance has both a narrower and a broader mission. While Conservancy is language agnostic and is focused on software development, The Alliance is focused exclusively on the C++ environment and is free to support non-FLOSS projects such as language proposals, books, education materials, tools, or other projects which may not be software or may have non-FLOSS licensing.

It is also the case that The C++ Alliance is not a good fit as a Conservancy project. It is appropriate for small non-proprietary projects to pool their resources to provide themselves with financial, legal, accounting, and other services for which they have a common need and for which they may, individually, be too small to economically procure. Although the Alliance will be promoting library development, it doesn't have the same requirements as a FLOSS software producer, so would need different services than your typical Conservancy project and has sufficient endowment to fund the services that it will require.

Michael R. Press
President
Professional Experience:

More than 25 years of experience in economic development and tax incentives, having served in both government and non-government consulting roles including:

Built and led the world's leading Business Incentives advisory practice while serving as Tax Partner at Ernst & Young.

Supervised incentives advisory engagements for Fortune 100 Companies in the aerospace, automotive, consumer products, financial services, telecommunications, energy, pharmaceuticals, food and beverage production and packaging industries.

Assistant Director of Tax Studies, Senate Finance Committee, New York State Senate (Majority);

Chief Economist and Commissioner (Acting) of the New York City Office of Business Development.

Chairman of the Enterprise Zone Administrative Boards of four Enterprise (EDZ's) in New York City.

Engagement highlights include:

Led the negotiating team that brought the Boeing Company's world headquarters from Seattle to Chicago (while considering Dallas and Denver).

Led the site selection and negotiating team in support of the largest industrial project of 2015 in Alabama.

Secured the benefits package sufficient to induce a 3,000-contingent Citigroup campus to relocate to New Jersey.

Secured first-of-its-kind incentive in the states of Connecticut, New Jersey, North Carolina, Florida and New York.

Created new and redesigned existing key economic development programs for New York City and New York State including; REAP (Relocation and Employee Assistance Program), ECSP (Energy Cost Savings Program), ICIP (Industrial and Commercial Incentive Program) and Tax Increment Financing.

Organized and hosted the first comprehensive private sector Business Incentives Symposium in the United States.

Education & Certifications:

M.S. - SUNY Albany, Economics

B.S. - SUNY Albany, Economics, Mathematics

Professional Associations & Affiliations:

Industrial Asset Management Council, Member

Journal of MultiState Taxation and Incentives, Editorial Board Member


Steve Weitzner
MANAGING DIRECTOR
Steve Weitzner has more than 30 years of experience in economic impact analysis, international location analysis and site selection, and economic development consulting. Steve worked for Coopers & Lybrand and Ernst & Young, helping both companies to establish and grow international and domestic site selection and incentives advisory service lines. He was also the founder and president of Silverlode Consulting, Inc., which is now owned and operated by GBX Group LLC based in Cleveland, OH.

 

While President of Silverlode for nearly 20 years, Steve developed the methodology for completion of comprehensive economic impact analyses of complex organizations and major proposed real estate developments. He was responsible for completing impact studies for entities such as Cleveland Clinic, University Hospitals, Baystate Health, Nemours, the Northeast Ohio Areawide Coordinating Agency (NOACA), the Ohio Department of Transportation, Ford Motor Company, General Motors, and many others.  

 

Steve is one of the most experienced economic development and site selection consultants in the US and has completed nearly 1,000 projects and analyses over the last three decades. He has worked on projects in 42 US states and in more than 25 countries. Since selling Silverlode in 2018, Steve works independently on a select number of projects each year and is an Adjunct Faculty member and Entrepreneur-in-Residence at Case Western Reserve University. He lives in Shaker Heights, Ohio with his wife and his dog, and is an avid squash player, skier, and French hornist.


Micah J. Press 
Senior Researcher
Micah Press is a policy, program and political researcher whose work spans environmental restoration, grassroots advocacy, human rights analysis, economic development and the study of public opinion and cultural dynamics. Before joining SCOUT Economics, Micah worked on large-scale environmental restoration and community-engagement projects, helping organizations build coalitions, mobilize local stakeholders, and communicate complex policy issues in clear, actionable terms.

Micah previously conducted human rights research for the United Nations by contributing to updates of the CIRI Human Rights Data Project, with a particular focus on torture, political imprisonment, and violations of physical integrity rights. This work required cross-national comparative analysis, rigorous methodological discipline, and careful handling of politically sensitive information.

Most recently, Micah completed a master’s thesis examinin g how political polarization and “post-truth” discourse manifest in online cultural spaces, using advanced qualitative coding and computational text-analysis tools to map emerging trends in public sentiment. His broader research interests include political communication, digital discourse, interpersonal polarization, and the relationship between culture and governance.

At SCOUT Economics, Micah supports research, analysis, dataroom management and team coordination across environmental, economic development, and public policy engagements. He brings a strong foundation in data synthesis, stakeholder communication, and narrative-driven analysis to help clients understand the social and political dynamics shaping their business environment.

Micah holds a Master of Arts in Political Science from the University of Connecticut. He lives in Cambridge, Massachusetts, where he works as a Risk Intelligence Analyst and spends his free time hiking, cooking, and critiquing film.

Review Text: Matthew Wilhite, Founder and Owner of Kingdom Strollers, partnered with Concepta to redesign and enhance his company’s website in response to a significant operational disruption caused by a policy change at Disney-owned resorts. Previously, the business relied on delivering rental strollers directly to resort luggage rooms, but the new policy required in-person handoffs with guests, introducing logistical complexity and potential disruption to customer experience. Concepta was tasked with quickly adapting the company’s digital infrastructure to accommodate this shift by developing a dynamic reservation system that allowed customers to pre-book stroller rentals with specific date- and location-based timeslots. The solution accounted for variability across different resorts and days of the week, ensuring operational feasibility while maintaining a seamless customer experience. Having worked with Concepta for several years, Matthew selected them based on their deep understanding of his business model, responsiveness, and consistent delivery quality. The project team included a coordinator and lead developers who collaborated closely with Matthew through in-person and virtual meetings, translating business requirements into effective technical solutions while also providing strategic suggestions to improve efficiency and clarity. As a result of this engagement, Kingdom Strollers was able to transition smoothly without disruption to its reservation process, proactively position itself ahead of competitors, and maintain its leadership in a niche market serving Orlando theme park visitors. Matthew emphasized Concepta’s exceptional availability, empathy, and commitment to understanding the business from the owner’s perspective, noting that they consistently go above and beyond to address urgent needs. The final product was highly user-friendly and aligned with real-world operational demands rather than purely technical considerations. Overall, the collaboration played a critical role in enabling business continuity, enhancing customer experience, and strengthening the company’s competitive advantage during a challenging transition period.

Reach-out Message: Hi Stephan, Leading GrapeStars while building a compliant multi-retailer e-commerce platform in a highly regulated industry reflects strong execution and strategic clarity. How did you ensure alignment between technical complexity and regulatory compliance across multiple states? Looking forward to connecting. Parag Google | Founder SoftwareBrio.com
Review Text: Stephan Fortier, Co-founder of GrapeStars International, engaged Concepta to develop a highly complex and large-scale mobile and web-based e-commerce platform after facing two unsuccessful attempts with other vendors. The project required building a robust system capable of supporting hundreds of retailers across the United States, each offering thousands of products with varying pricing structures, all while operating under strict U.S. liquor laws and the three-tier distribution system. The platform needed to ensure daily financial batching into individual retailer accounts and maintain compliance with varying state regulations. Additionally, multiple third-party integrations were required, including Stripe for payments, Signifyd for fraud protection, ShipEngine for logistics, Narvar for customer engagement, and Intercom for communication. Concepta deployed a well-structured and scalable team, initially consisting of a Director of Operations, Project Manager, and frontend and backend developers, later expanding to include a team lead, QA specialist, and designer to meet high design expectations. The workflow was highly efficient, involving bi-weekly meetings, detailed sprint planning, and structured communication through spreadsheets outlining tasks, timelines, and backlogs. Despite initial communication challenges, the team adapted quickly and significantly improved performance. The final product was a visually stunning, fully functional platform that successfully met all regulatory and technical requirements. Stephan highlighted Concepta’s professionalism, problem-solving capabilities, accountability, and efficiency as standout qualities. He emphasized that the team minimized wasted effort, resolved issues permanently, and delivered exceptional value given the project’s complexity, ultimately describing them as a rare and reliable partner in an industry filled with inconsistent providers.

Review Text: Orkut Buyukkokten, CEO and Co-founder of Hello Network, oversees the development of a social networking platform designed to foster meaningful human connections in a digital-first world. The QuarkWorks team integrated closely with Hello Network’s internal developers, participating in agile workflows, weekly sprint planning, and daily development tasks. Their engineers demonstrated strong ownership, often exceeding expectations by proactively suggesting new features and improvements, even outside standard working hours. Leadership from QuarkWorks maintained consistent communication and transparency, enabling a highly collaborative and efficient development environment. Over time, this partnership evolved into a deeply integrated relationship where QuarkWorks functioned as an extension of the internal team rather than a traditional outsourced vendor. The engagement, which has exceeded a million dollars in investment, delivered consistently high-quality, maintainable code and contributed significantly to the platform’s growth. Orkut’s organization valued their professionalism, adaptability, and startup-like agility, noting that access to a broader pool of expertise within QuarkWorks added exceptional value. Review Text: James Hirchak, Founder and President of Astute Review, partnered with Alligatortek to develop a custom productivity software plugin designed to enhance efficiency within Microsoft PowerPoint by automating repetitive formatting and quality control tasks. The initiative stemmed from a clear need to reduce time spent on low-value, manual processes that often burden professionals in consulting, marketing, and corporate environments. The solution aimed to streamline workflows by enabling automated checks for spelling, formatting inconsistencies, and brand compliance within presentations. The engagement began with a detailed discovery and planning phase, during which James and his team collaborated closely with Alligatortek through weekly meetings to define requirements, validate ideas, and ensure alignment on product vision. While Astute Review contributed initial wireframes and user experience direction, Alligatortek took full ownership of the technical development, leveraging their structured “blueprint builder” methodology to translate concepts into a robust and scalable product. The team composition included a project manager and multiple developers who maintained consistent communication and demonstrated strong responsiveness throughout the development lifecycle. With an investment of approximately $345,000, the resulting solution was a powerful and high-quality software tool that effectively addressed the intended challenges. James highlighted Alligatortek’s exceptional attention to detail, particularly in how the project manager meticulously defined each requirement and ensured nothing was overlooked during development. Although there were initial concerns around delivery speed and accuracy, the team successfully met expectations and delivered a reliable product. Post-launch, Alligatortek continued to provide maintenance and updates under a retainer model; however, turnaround times during this phase were noted to be slower due to involvement of an offshore team. Despite this, the overall experience remained highly positive, with James emphasizing the importance of trusting the development process and acknowledging that the final outcome justified both the cost and timeline, ultimately resulting in a valuable and impactful productivity solution.

Felicia highly commended Zetaton for their exceptional transparency, organized sprint-based project management, and ability to execute ahead of schedule. The revamp successfully improved user experience by reducing the mental load of task management, providing a cleaner UI that received positive feedback from the platform's user base during the initial phase of deployment.

© Tend Task, Inc.Relationship Equity Coach • Researcher • Master Celebrant
I See the Pattern. I Name It. Then We Change It.
I’m Felicia Kashevaroff. Founder of Tend Task, certified relationship coach, active researcher in modern partnership dynamics, and the person who will say the thing no one else will say, and then help you do something about it.


Why "Tend Task"?
The name reflects a philosophy, not just a methodology.

Tend is the emotional labor — the nurturing, attunement, and care that keeps two people genuinely connected. For too long, this has been treated as one person’s job.

Task is the cognitive labor — the planning, anticipating, and managing of a shared life. For too long, this has also been treated as one person’s job.

When both partners fully share both — not just the tasks, but the thinking, the noticing, and the caring — that’s when a partnership becomes something exceptional. That’s what we build here.

The Work Started With My Own Story
I know this work because I’ve lived it. I spent years in an unequal partnership carrying the invisible labor — the mental load, the emotional weight, the planning and anticipating and managing that nobody sees or names. My partner’s career flourished. Mine stalled. His dreams expanded. Mine quietly shrank. It happened slowly, without my conscious understanding — the way these patterns always do.

That experience sent me on a mission. I broke the pattern, rebuilt from the ground up, and then spent years researching exactly why it happens — to me, to couples who love each other, to people who genuinely believe in equality. What I found: good intentions aren’t enough. The conditioning runs deeper than words can reach. You can’t communicate your way out of unconscious bias.

Traditional relationship prep doesn’t mention the mental load. Couples therapy rarely addresses the unconscious patterns or structural forces driving the imbalance. The books collect dust while the patterns calcify. I built Tend Task because I refuse to let you lose yourself the way I did — and because I know exactly what it takes to build something different.

Book an Intro with Felicia
As Seen In










The Tend Task Framework:
Root + System + Ritual
Most approaches to relationship work give you surface-level tools and hope they stick. I go deeper. Every engagement with Tend Task is built on three layers:

Root
You can’t change what you can’t see. We uncover the unconscious beliefs, inherited patterns, and gendered conditioning that are shaping your relationship without your consent — and replace them with something you’ve actually chosen.

System
Good intentions aren’t enough. You need actual frameworks for sharing the mental load, making decisions equitably, and sustaining balance under pressure — when you’re exhausted, overwhelmed, or navigating a major life transition. We build those systems together.

Ritual
Systems keep things equal. Rituals keep you connected. We design meaningful practices — from daily touchstones to the ceremony itself — that carry your values into the reality of life together and remind you why you chose this partnership.

Credentials & Training
The Tend Task methodology combines cutting-edge relationship science with practical, real-world solutions. My work is grounded in:

Advanced Certifications & Training
ICF Certified Relationship Coach
Master Celebrant & Ceremony Designer
Gottman Method Trained Professional
Organization and Relationship Systems (ORSC) Certified
Conflict Management Specialist
Active researcher in modern partnership dynamics

Current Research
Understanding Modern Partnership Dynamics
I’m currently leading a groundbreaking study examining how high-achieving individuals navigate modern relationships, with insights from over 80 participants and growing. My research reveals key findings about partnership dynamics in ambitious relationships.

Key Research Areas & Findings

Power Dynamics & Gender Roles
Our research shows that modern couples are actively challenging traditional relationship structures, developing unique strategies for maintaining equity. While many couples are making conscious efforts to create balance, we’ve found that mental load disparities persist, particularly in heterosexual partnerships – even when both partners are high achievers.


Impact of Proactive Support
A significant finding shows that 45% of participants have engaged in couples therapy, with the most successful outcomes occurring when couples use it proactively rather than reactively. This insight has deeply informed our approach to relationship coaching and premarital work.


The Ambition Factor
Our research reveals a distinct “ambition gap” affecting partnership dynamics. High achievers often struggle to find partners who match their drive for growth and success, leading to unique challenges in building equal partnerships that support both individuals’ aspirations


Emerging Insights
The study has uncovered fascinating patterns around neurodiversity in relationships, the impact of proactive relationship support, and the evolution of power dynamics in modern partnerships. These findings directly inform our coaching methodologies and tools.

*Research participants include diverse relationship stages (57% married, 17% single, 8% partnered, 5% separated, 10% divorced, 3% engaged) and family structures (63% with children, 10% LGBTQIA+).*

Ready to Build Something Different?
You’ve seen the pattern. You know something needs to change. Let’s figure out where to start.

On Air Warning

On Air Warning was founded by Michael Vitiello in October, 2020. Vitiello has been an entrepreneur all his life, and currently runs a successful business selling laptop computers into the education market.

The pandemic changed the structure of the Vitiello household. Overnight, the kitchen and dining room turned into video conference stations. Mrs. Vitiello, a teacher, was conducting classes from home. Their two teenagers were attending classes, and two older daughters were managing careers from home. It was a challenging environment because no one knew when the others were in a meeting or engaged in a conversation.

The idea for a device that would let everyone know each other’s status was born while walking by his wife’s desk, numerous times a day, trying to see from a distance if Zoom was opened and if the Camera was on, or the Mic muted. “I spent hours searching the internet for a solution” says Vitiello, “There was no automated device that warns you when a microphone or camera is on.” It didn’t take long for him to reach out to some trusted friends and pull together a team to develop the software and design the solution.

“Before we had the first prototype in hand, anyone that heard about its functionality, wanted one” said Vitiello. “We knew we had a winner when everyone we talked to told us a story about how On Air Warning would have saved them, or someone they know from an embarrassing moment.” Since then, so many people have said “Why didn’t I think of that?”

Review Text: Spencer Ware, Co-Founder of REPOWR, utilized Tyrannosaurus Tech to architect and develop a sophisticated asset-sharing platform designed to allow logistics firms to seamlessly manage and share equipment across distributed networks. Tyrannosaurus Tech provided a comprehensive suite of services including full-stack web development, proactive project management, and rigorous quality assurance. By integrating directly into REPOWR's internal workflows, the Tyrannosaurus Tech team played a pivotal role in the production of the platform from initial concept to final deployment. The engagement was characterized by a high degree of accountability, where Tyrannosaurus Tech managed coaching and technical oversight internally rather than shifting the burden to the client. This collaboration resulted in a robust, high-performance software product that significantly enhanced equipment visibility for 3rd-party trailer interchanges. The partnership has been ongoing since early 2022, with the service provider consistently delivering high-quality, scalable code that supports REPOWR’s mission to modernize B2B trailer interchange management through real-time insights and digital chains of custody.
linkedin company about:
With REPOWR, asset-based fleets, brokers, shippers, and owner-operators can tap into the the platform to seamlessly exchange trailer capacity.

The platform supplements existing fleet management tools and empowers logistics companies to fully leverage the benefits of a trailers-as-a-service model.

Fleets supplying trailers to the network are able to optimize trailer utilization, maximize revenue per trailer, and reduce empty mileage. Fleets also accrue a variety of other efficiency gains by leveraging REPOWR’s streamlined interchange experience — certificate of insurance verification, dynamic trailer pricing, and digital chains of custody.

Simultaneously, logistics companies seeking trailers can scale with ease and efficiency as the platform offers on-demand access to trailers under a flexible pay-per-use model.

Sharing trailers creates a win-win experience, helping both parties mitigate risks associated with market volatility.

Frequently asked questions
Why is a REPOWR trailer better than traditional leasing or ownership? 
add
Most folks will tell you that if you don't own a trailer, you can't grow. But getting buried in debt or stuck in a restrictive long-term lease just to get moving is a trap. REPOWR changes that. Instead of signing your life away on a massive loan for a trailer you might not need next month, we give you access to the equipment you need right now—and the flexibility to drop it when you need to pick up a drop & hook. You get the hauling power of a massive fleet while keeping the freedom of running your own show.

‍

How REPOWR works for your business:
‍

Why are these trailers available?
Fleets use REPOWR to reposition equipment or maintain utilization of idle trailers. You can book these units to handle your own freight and deliver them where they are needed.
‍
Why is it more cost-effective than leasing?
No Long-Term Contracts: Traditional leases lock you into 12–36 month commitments; REPOWR offers daily, weekly, or monthly flexibility with no long-term baggage.
Eliminate "Dead Time": You only pay for the equipment when you have a load to pull, effectively eliminating the overhead of a lease payment when your truck isn't moving.
No Long-Term Debt: Traditional leases trap you in 12–36 month contracts. REPOWR gives you the flexibility to rent by the job so you’re never stuck with a payment you don't want.
Flexible Terms: You can book a trailer for as little as 3 days or keep it for up to 90 days depending on the unit. (Note: New users have a 30-day limit until they finish their probationary period).
Easy Extensions: If your job runs long, you can request an extension through the platform. Just make sure to send your request at least 7 days before your drop-off date to avoid a late-extension fee.
30-Day Billing Cycles: Payments cover your current 30-day window. To avoid being charged for the following month, simply return the trailer before your next billing cycle begins.

The Bottom Line: You get the equipment you need to move your freight without the long-term baggage of a lease. When your project is over, return the trailer to end your obligation for all future billing cycles.
Why is it better than owning?
Debt-Free Scaling: You can scale your capacity up or down instantly without taking on high-interest equipment loans or balance-sheet liability.
No Major Repair Bills: When you own, you’re responsible for the long-term wear and tear, major overhauls, and the value of the trailer dropping over time. With us, the owner handles the big, long-term maintenance. You’re just responsible for the trailer while it’s in your possession—and for some listings, basic maintenance is already included in your rate.
Ready When You Need It: Every trailer is expected to be road-ready when you pick it up. You don’t have to worry about the costs of storing equipment or keeping it maintained during the slow seasons when it's just sitting in a yard. 
The "Virtual Fleet" Advantage: Access thousands of  trailers nationwide, giving you the freedom to pick up a job anywhere in the country without being tethered to your home yard.

The Bottom Line: You get to use the trailer to make money without the headaches of owning it. You handle the hauling, the owner handles the long-term upkeep, and you keep your business flexible.
What do I need to get started on REPOWR for the first time?
add
Answer: Entering the REPOWR marketplace is designed to be as fast as our booking process. While we maintain an elite network, getting your account "road-ready" only requires a few standard industry credentials. Because security is engineered into our onboarding, your initial setup ensures you can move from "searching" to "hauling" without the typical administrative back-and-forth.

Your First-Time Setup Checklist:

Account Creation:
Create your account with your Highway.com login and we’ll verify your individual identity to ensure a secure, fraud-free experience on our platform.
Authority Verification:
Most suppliers require at least 6 months of continuous DOT authority.
Insurance Documentation (COI):
You will need to upload a Certificate of Insurance that includes:
$1M Auto Liability.
Trailer Interchange coverage (typically $40k for Dry Vans; $50k-$60k for Reefers).
Additionally Insured policy for the certificate holder
One-Tap Reservation Power:
Once your credentials are in the system, you can search and reserve assets in 90 seconds without needing a phone call.
‍

‍

Can I change my drop-off location mid-trip?
add
One of the core benefits of the REPOWR marketplace is the agility it provides your business. Markets shift and high-paying loads can emerge in unexpected lanes, and our platform supports adjustments when needed. Return locations are based on the supplier’s listed drop yard and may only be changed at the supplier’s discretion and with approval. This ensures flexibility while maintaining alignment and equipment control.

How to adjust your destination mid-trip:

Requesting a Change:
Navigate to your active reservation and request a change to any supplier-supported location.
Supplier Approval:
Since every move is an engineered workflow, the asset owner must approve the new destination to ensure it aligns with their repositioning needs.
Financial Adjustments:
Higher Rates: If the new location has a higher daily market rate, your billing will be adjusted to reflect the difference for the remainder of the trip.
No Refunds: If the new location is in a lower-demand area, the original rate remains in effect.
‍

Can I book multiple trailers for a large project?
add
Answer: The REPOWR marketplace is designed to be a high-volume capacity engine. While individual carriers use us to stay independent, we provide specialized tools for brokers and 3PLs to orchestrate complex, nationwide trailer projects with ease. By moving away from manual procurement and fragmented tracking, any logistics professional can now build, manage, and monitor a custom "mini-fleet" through a single, secure dashboard.

How we power large-scale execution:

Intelligent Procurement for Brokers & 3PLs:
Instant Quotes: Get immediate trailer cost estimates to help you provide competitive proposals to your shippers.
Project Requests: Submit volume-based requests for bids or active business and receive a specialized capacity response within 48 hours.
Bulk Reservations for Scaling Carriers:
If a listing shows high quantity availability, any qualified user can reserve multiple units in a single workflow to instantly expand their footprint.
Carrier Assignment & Management:
Brokers can seamlessly assign trailers to their invited carriers, who are already vetted with automated insurance and payment setup.
Easily reassign trailers as project needs shift, ensuring your assets are always where they generate the most value.
Project Monitoring & Chain of Custody:
Maintain a full history of every trailer's journey with standardized pickup and drop-off inspections.
Track your fleet in real-time via 36+ telematics integrations (like Samsara and Spireon) to hold the right parties accountable without the liability of ownership.
Project Close-out:
Once a project is complete, handle all returns and final documentation within the platform to ensure a clean handoff back to the asset owner.
‍

Trailer Owners: How do I control where my trailers end up?
add
Answer: We understand that your assets are your livelihood. REPOWR isn't a "free-for-all" marketplace; it's a strategic repositioning tool designed to put you in the driver's seat of your fleet’s movement. Through our "digital gatekeeper" approach, you can automate the flow of your equipment without ever losing the final right of refusal, ensuring your trailers always end up exactly where your business needs them next.

Your Strategic Control Tools:

Pre-Defined Destinations:
When listing your equipment, you define the only eligible drop-off locations available to renters.
Renter Restrictions:
The platform prevents users from selecting any destination you haven't explicitly approved during the listing phase.
Final Approval Power:
Even if a carrier meets all criteria, every reservation is sent to you for a final digital sign-off before the execution phase begins.
Trailer Owners: What protection do I have for my equipment?
add
Answer: Trust is the currency of our marketplace. Because security is engineered into every step—not bolted on—we’ve removed the "guesswork" of renting out your assets. From rigorous identity vetting to automated insurance verification, we ensure that every carrier picking up your trailer is a verified professional who meets the highest industry standards for compliance and safety.

How We Protect Your Assets:

Automated COI Verification:
Before any renter picks up an asset, our system confirms a valid Certificate of Insurance (COI) is on file.
Certificate Holder Status:
REPOWR ensures you are listed as the Certificate Holder on the renter's policy for the duration of the move.
Standardized Coverage Limits:
We enforce strict Trailer Interchange minimums (typically $40k for Dry Vans; $50k-$60k for Reefers) to cover non-owned equipment under the carrier's care.
Vetting Standards:
Powered by Highway, we decline 15-25% of carriers who don't meet our rigorous safety benchmarks before they can even access your listings.

Why I started Vivo
Vivo
/ ˈviːvəʊ /; adjective

definition: Italian for “lively”, a musical term that means to play music with life and vigor

Vivo began with my parents. As I watched them age, I saw firsthand how the loss of strength and mobility impacted their independence and quality of life. It was heartbreaking to see them struggle with everyday tasks, and I knew I had to help.​

I discovered that while muscle loss is inevitable as we age, it doesn’t have to define us. Strength training can reverse this decline, improving balance, mobility, and overall health. Inspired by my parents’ journey, I left a 25-year career in corporate America to create Vivo – a program designed to help them, and others stay strong, confident, and independent.​

Now, Vivo has become a family affair. My parents, my children, and I take Vivo classes together every week, even though we live in different states. It’s a way to stay connected, have fun, and prioritize our health as a family. ​

At Vivo, we’re not just about exercise – we’re about empowering people to live better, healthier lives.​

— Eric​

Our mission is to celebrate and elevate Tucson’s vibrant food and beverage culture by connecting people, sharing stories, and supporting the local culinary community. Through engaging content, inclusive events, and a passion for what makes our city unique, we aim to inspire a deeper appreciation for good food, great people, and the rich traditions that bring us all to the table.

All of our content—from curated "Best Of" guides to breaking news to  feature stories—is free to enjoy. We focus on the positive, showcasing the culinary gems of Tucson and the stories behind them.

We host dozens of events, including Sonoran Restaurant Week, Vegan Night Market, Tucson Craft Beer Crawl, and more.

Follow us on Instagram and Facebook, subscribe to our weekly newsletter, or—if you're a true foodie—join our club to explore local restaurants and enjoy unique foodie experiences. As a member, you’ll get exclusive benefits, including gift cards to restaurants, to festivals, and access to our special R&D dinners and member mixers.

Our vision
We aim to democratize access to skilled trades training, accelerate the global creation of jobs, and ensure stable infrastructure.

In less than a decade, smartphones, tablets, and apps entirely reshaped how we live, work, and communicate. Today, we are using them to make workforce development and safety training more accessible than ever before.

Our purpose
Our goal is to become the gold standard in field-based construction education by delivering the most cutting-edge development and safety training.

By creating an app with thousands of safety and training videos, we’re ensuring the transfer of knowledge that will prevent delayed projects, accidents, and a lack of regulatory compliance.

About CAHill TECH
In 2019, Carley Hill took all the knowledge she gained working for her family’s construction company to create a business of her own, CAHill TECH. Through its award-winning aQuiRe app, CAHill TECH is training the next generation of construction workers in a way they’re already familiar with – by watching videos on their phones.

This company wide safety and training platform was created as a solution to an urgent construction problem. As baby boomers retire, construction companies struggle to attract and train the next generation of workers.

That’s where the aQuiRe app comes in. It is designed for the front-line workers and contains thousands of safety and training videos, from how to correctly read a tape measure to pouring concrete. The CAHill team adds new modules regularly while at the same time creating custom content to meet the specific needs of leading US construction companies.

Our objective is to continue working towards democratizing access to these trades-based skills and allow everyone with motivation and drive to be a part of the construction industry.

Meet the industry experts behind CAHill

I am an attorney in private practice focused on the preservation and protection of individual's rights from governmental abuse. I trained at a trial-focused law school and began my career at one of the leading criminal defense firms in the country with a proven record of effective, aggressive, and innovative indigent client representation. The Miami-Dade Office of the Public Defender was and is a training ground for many of my legal heroes; I am more than proud of my relationship with that office, which continues to this day.

We are in it together. 
      

I provide INTERPOL and criminal advocacy for clients who value their reputations, liberty, and resources.

Our History
The Curtis family has been in the restaurant supply industry since 1945. Dick Curtis set out on his own in 1986 to establish Market Source, Inc., and has provided Oklahoma with top quality food service supplies ever since.

Hours of Operation
Nearby? Come shop our HUGE 60,000+ square foot showroom 8AM-5PM, Monday- Friday.

Customers First
At Market Source, we do things differently. First, we make our customers our top priority and always have. This means friendly, helpful service during every step of your experience. Forget pushy sales people or long waits to get the products you want. We have what you need, when you need it, and we’ll do our best to ensure you find exactly what you’re looking for.

CEO DICK CURTIS Founder & CEO
 
Huge Showroom!
How do we do it? We start with a newly expanded 60,000+ square foot showroom containing more than 10,000+ items in stock.
Showroom
Massive Inventory In Stock
Our warehouse and inventory are the biggest in the state! From cooking equipment to kitchenware, furniture to refrigeration, we carry all the top brands and we know our products. We have it when you need it! Whether you need Oneida silverware and Homer Laughlin China, or a Saturn Cooking Unit or Refrigerator, our educated staff is ready to help. We keep continuous stock on thousands of items to eliminate a delay to your busy kitchen service. Can't find what you need? We can help you order it!

Equipment Showroom
 
 
Self-Service
When you walk into Market Source, you can walk out the same day with everything you need. Our self-service retail style gives you the power to see our products up close, make educated decisions and leave with everything in hand. Not to worry, we have experienced and knowledgable staff ready to help at any time! 

Professional Services
Whether you’re updating an existing business or starting from scratch, Market Source offers professional services to complement our quality products. These include:

Layout, Kitchen and Interior Design Assistance
 

Personalized Site Visits
 

Expert Consultations
 

Custom Fabrication
 

Special Orders

Review Text: Forix partnered with Gingerbread Construction Company, a family-owned business operating both brick-and-mortar locations and a nationwide e-commerce platform, to completely modernize and scale their online store using Magento 2.0. The engagement was driven by the need to replace an outdated system that had been in place since 2009 and relied heavily on legacy backend infrastructure, which created operational risk and limited scalability. Forix undertook a full redevelopment of the website from scratch, upgrading both frontend and backend systems while retaining only essential content elements from the previous version. They implemented robust integrations with tools such as ShipperHQ for real-time shipping calculations and ShipStation for backend order fulfillment and label management, addressing the company’s complex logistics challenges involving perishable goods and multi-address shipping requirements. The team introduced streamlined workflows, automated processes, and improved inventory and asset tracking features, significantly enhancing operational efficiency. Their structured and meticulous project management approach, facilitated through Basecamp, ensured clear communication, organized task tracking, and alignment across multiple time zones, enabling rapid turnaround cycles and consistent progress. The results were substantial, with the company experiencing its busiest November on record, a significant increase in new customer acquisition, and notable growth in corporate sales driven by improved search visibility and a more professional online presence. Forix maintained strong adherence to timelines and budget constraints while providing strategic guidance throughout the process, challenging assumptions when necessary rather than passively executing requests. Their collaborative approach, combined with exceptional communication and a deep understanding of e-commerce systems, resulted in a highly successful platform transformation. While minor improvements could be made in clarifying scope boundaries related to third-party integrations, the team consistently went beyond expectations to ensure smooth implementation and client satisfaction, making them a trusted long-term partner in the company’s digital evolution.

Our Process

At Byproduct Connect, we develop byproduct management programs and sustainable sourcing solutions to bridge the manufacturing, construction, mining, energy, and agricultural industries.

We reduce disposal costs, generate revenue and create cost-efficient sourcing solutions.

Our process includes:

01
Analyzing Material Streams
Unlocking value with our materials science expertise

Evaluate the characterization, volume, and origin of byproduct streams or purchasing needs to identify opportunities for industrial reuse.

02
Connecting to Global Network
Your place in the industrial ecosystem

Match your materials in Byproduct Connect's network of 1,000+ materials, markets, and logistics partners.

03
Coordinating Logistics
Cost-Efficient Transportation

Efficiently manage the storage, packaging, and transportation of materials (via truck, rail, or ocean barge) to ensure low-cost and sustainable delivery.

04
Tracking Impact Metrics
Measure Success, Sustainably and Profitably

Monitor the economic, environmental, and lifecycle benefits through detailed Scope 3 reporting and analytics.

Our Services
Explore our tailored materials solutions designed to help industries transform waste into valuable resources.


Circular Waste Audits
Unlock the potential of your waste with our Circular Waste Audit & Material Analysis. We sort and separate materials from office and municipality buildings, then apply materials science expertise to evaluate their properties. We track diversion rates and identify the best reuse scenarios (industrial feedstock, recycling, reuse, composting), ensuring zero-waste and LEED compliance. Through economic analysis, we highlight cost savings via improved recycling. From insights to execution, we align your operations with environmental and economic needs.

Includes: Material Sorting, Diversion Rates, Material and Economic Analysis, Reuse Opportunities, LEED Credits

The Asian Legacy Library is a global non-profit organization dedicated to safeguarding the cultural literary wisdom traditions of the world.
We are working to unite the best of technology and library science in order to keep wisdom alive.

The Asian Legacy Library is home to some of the rarest cultural wisdom literature in existence. We have digitally documented many of these priceless treasures which still exist in collections around the globe.


Our digital library is the flagship destination and access point for our preservation programs. It is the nexus of source literature used by countless scholars, translators, and teachers.

All money raised or received by the Asian Legacy Library is used solely for the purpose of supporting its mission to locate, digitally preserve, and safeguard precious collections of cultural wisdom literature.

Review Text: Gustavo Tolone, while serving as CTO of The Assemblage, partnered with noformat to build the company’s entire brand identity and digital ecosystem from the ground up during its early-stage growth. The engagement, spanning from mid-2016 to 2019 with a total investment between $700,000 and $1 million, involved comprehensive services including branding, web development, mobile app development, and graphic design. noformat created a fully integrated digital presence, developing a WordPress-based website with PHP, React, and Node.js, along with Android and iOS mobile applications built using React Native. They also implemented API integrations for member management systems and developed a community blog to enhance user engagement. Beyond digital products, noformat designed all physical branding assets such as signage, printed materials, and environmental graphics across multiple coworking properties in New York City. The results were highly successful, with the platform supporting nearly 3,000 members and receiving widespread positive feedback from users and stakeholders. The website was also recognized as one of the top coworking space websites by industry evaluators. Gustavo highlighted noformat’s exceptional creativity, professionalism, and research-driven approach, noting their ability to deeply understand client needs and consistently deliver high-quality, innovative solutions. Their project management was highly structured and efficient, utilizing tools like Slack and Asana, maintaining frequent communication through daily or weekly meetings, and delivering most milestones on time. While their pricing was acknowledged as premium, Gustavo emphasized that the quality of work justified the investment, stating that their efficiency and output far exceeded that of lower-cost alternatives. Overall, the collaboration stands out as a benchmark example of strategic branding and full-scale digital product development executed at a high level.

Review Text: Roberta Kelley, CEO and Founder of REAME LLC, partnered with Varfaj to develop a mobile work order application tailored for the real estate property management sector. The engagement, which took place between July 2021 and January 2022 with an investment ranging between $20,000 and $50,000, focused on building a user-friendly platform that enables tenants and maintenance teams to efficiently submit, track, and manage work orders. Varfaj delivered a comprehensive solution, developing the app initially for iOS with plans for Android expansion, while also supporting marketing efforts to successfully launch the product in the App Store and introduce it to the market. Roberta emphasized the team’s exceptional professionalism, patience, and ability to guide her through the technical aspects of the project, especially given her non-technical background. The Varfaj team maintained strong communication via Slack, consistently delivered work on time, and demonstrated transparency by clearly explaining any delays or challenges encountered during development. One of the most notable aspects of the engagement was their personalized approach, ensuring that Roberta felt valued and supported rather than treated as just another client. Their ability to simplify complex processes, provide hands-on guidance, and maintain a collaborative working relationship significantly contributed to the successful delivery and launch of the app. Overall, the partnership stands out for its strong project management, customer-centric approach, and effective execution in bringing a real estate tech idea to life.

About
 
Springrates
Springrates® has been the trusted source for performance suspension parts since 2005, specializing in coilovers, lowering springs, and air suspension kits from top brands. Built by enthusiasts, for enthusiasts, we’re dedicated to helping you enhance handling, ride quality, and stance—backed by expert support and competitive prices.



20 YEARS IN THE INDUSTRY

We're the Original Suspension Source
Springrates is your go-to destination for lowering and upgrading your vehicle's performance. With a focus on coilovers and lowering springs, we cater to drivers who value precision handling, a sleek stance, and a better driving experience. Our team brings deep product knowledge and personalized service to every order, helping you build the perfect suspension setup.

Background
At Springrates, we’re more than just a suspension retailer; we’re a community of enthusiasts driven by a deep-rooted passion for cars. That passion was passed down by our founder's father and helped spark a journey that shaped Springrates into what it is today.

Our story begins in 2001, when our founder took his first job as a web designer for an aftermarket body kit company that was an official NOPI Tour stop. There, he blended technical and creative skills while producing websites and designing ads for major publications like Super Street magazine. It was the perfect mix of passion and purpose, leading to the launch of Springrates in 2005 with a clear focus that will never go out of style: performance suspension.

After the sudden passing of his father, Sean, our founder, felt a spark ignite within him—a determination to follow his dream and go all-in on building Springrates. In 2014, he revitalized the company with a renewed mission: to serve the automotive community with transparency, integrity, and genuine support. What began as a passion project has since grown into a trusted name in suspension, yet Springrates remains rooted in the same small-business values that sparked its journey—personal service, honesty, and an unwavering commitment to the car community.

Why Suspension Matters
Upgrading your suspension is one of the most impactful ways to improve your vehicle’s performance and aesthetics. Lowering your car with high-quality coilovers or lowering springs reduces body roll, improves cornering, and enhances braking response—resulting in a sharper, more responsive ride. Compared to soft, comfort-focused factory setups, aftermarket suspension delivers the ideal blend of performance handling and aggressive stance.

What Sets Springrates Apart?
Springrates.com offers one of the best shopping experiences in the industry with fast shipping, competitive pricing, and top-rated customer service. Most in-stock products ship within 24 hours, and orders over $250 include free shipping in the Continental USA. We’re transparent about inventory and lead times, so you know exactly what to expect. As a small team of enthusiasts, we’re here to help you choose the best suspension for your car—whether you're daily driving, tracking, or tuning for show.

To Each Their Low
"To Each Their Low" is more than a phrase; it’s a mindset that embraces the individuality of every build. Whether you're dialing in stance, chasing lap times, or perfecting a show car look, we support your vision. Our wide range of premium suspension options empowers you to achieve your ideal ride height, performance, and style—your car, your low, your way. We foster a positive tuning culture that celebrates creativity and personal enjoyment, and discourages negativity.

Why "Springrates"?
The name “Springrates” reflects our dedication to precision suspension tuning. Spring rates refer to how stiff or soft a spring is—a core element in how your car rides and handles. Getting it right means striking a perfect balance between comfort, control, and performance. With expert guidance and a curated selection of products, we help you fine-tune your suspension to meet your exact goals.

Springrates.com is more than a parts store. It’s a hub for enthusiasts who want to lower their cars the right way, improve their handling, and connect with a community that shares their passion. We’re here to help you unlock your car’s full potential—on the street, the track, or anywhere in between.

Importance of Proper Asphalt Compaction
When we drive the roads of our neighborhoods, we want smooth driving, not potholes or cracks. As a pavement contractor, we know these signs of early failure are due to poor compaction.

​

Typically, quality control of pavement involves spot checking with a nuclear gauge or core sampling – which often gives us the density information too late for the work to be improved.


The ideal solution is an accurate real time density reading during construction. We found that RTDensity™ does just that by putting the power of process control in the hands of the roller operator with real-time compaction estimates, and profiles for mat temperatures, speed data, pass counts and mat coverage.

ABOUT THE COMMERCIAL CO-OP
The Commercial Co-Op is a real estate technology company that was built by commercial real estate brokers familiar with all the problems of existing RE tech platforms starting with way too expensive and not delivering on what a Commercial Real Estate company really needs. There is so much redundancy in the tasks Commercial Real Estate Brokers perform when it takes multiple platforms to do the work. These redundant tasks cost time and lots of money plus the professional broker can end up with conflicting materials and bad information.

We created the Commercial Co-Op to streamline the day-to-day work flow of a Commercial Broker and to connect the brokerage community better which benefits the client as well as the Broker.

As a small commercial firm based in Denver with offices in Grand Junction that refused to pay broker tech costs totaling $2,000 per month and more. We built this product for US and everyone in the Commercial Brokerage Industry.

Jay Soneff
Founder / COB / Lead Investor / Commercial Broker
Jay Soneff was born and raised in Denver, Colorado. The third generation Denverite attended John F. Kennedy High School. He earned a Division 1 baseball scholarship to The University of Colorado under Coach Irv Brown. In four years, Jay graduated with a Business Degree in Finance and Real Estate with a minor in Economics.

Jay was the first to take advantage of the new real estate commission that allowed for a real estate degree to replace the two years of Salesman status requirement for broker licensing. At age 22, Jay was the youngest broker in the state. Jay went back to school and received his Master’s degree in Construction Finance and Management from University of Denver in 1995.

As a developer, agent and investor in multiple real estate platforms, Jay has tremendous experience dealing with a variety of commercial development, investment, leasing and management situations.

Despite minor early-stage challenges, the team effectively adapted and delivered a high-quality platform consistent with the defined scope and expectations. Their ability to combine thoughtful product development with strong interpersonal collaboration stood out, with particular praise for their commitment to understanding the underlying business logic and translating it into an effective technical solution. The final outcome was a well-structured, scalable, and user-friendly platform that laid the foundation for RoomZoom’s growth in the competitive real estate and roommate matching space.

What is SHWASH?
SHWASH is a mobile on-demand car detailing service. We strive to provide the most convenient, reliable and transparent service to allow you to never have to worry about how you’re going to keep your car clean again. We bring the detail right to your doorstep, at the click of a button.
How do I book an appointment?
The process is simple. It starts with entering your address so we know where we will be detailing the vehicle, selecting the detailing package that best suits your needs,  followed by choosing a convenient day/time for you based on our availability for your area. Once the appointment is booked and confirmed, Your Detailer will come to your preferred location to provide the service on the day/time you requested. Rest assured, SHWASH securely handles your payment and chargers your card only after the service is completed, ensuring a secure transaction.
Do I need to call to get a quote first?
No you don’t! At SHWASH, we offer up-front and cost-effective pricing for our car detailing services. We have preset - fully transparent pricing via our select detail packages we offer. No matter the condition of your vehicle, there is a package that will fit your needs. If you are unsure which package to select, you can always contact us and we can recommend one for you based on your vehicle condition and what you want done :)
Do I need to book online?
All bookings are made online at https://book.shwash.co/. If you have a unique circumstance or would like someone on our team to book your appointment for you, please give us a call and we can assist you.
How do I pay?
Payment is done on the platform after entering your address, selecting your package, choosing the day/time of the appointment and providing your details. We accept both debit and credit cards for secure payment, ensuring utmost safety and security. Rest assured, SHWASH securely handles your payment and charges your card only after the service is completed, ensuring a secure transaction.

A multimedia division of the Annenberg Foundation, explore™ is a multimedia organization that champions the selfless acts of others, documenting leaders around the world who have devoted their lives to extraordinary causes. Both educational and inspirational, explore films cover topics from animal rights, health and human services, and poverty to the environment, education, and spirituality. explore.org also hosts one of the largest online networks of live HD nature and animal cameras.

Specialties
Documentary films, photography, and inspiration and knowledgeAbout Us
 


explore is a multimedia organization that documents leaders around the world who have devoted their lives to extraordinary causes. Both educational and inspirational, explore creates a portal into the soul of humanity by championing the selfless acts of others.

explore’s growing library consists of more than 250 original films and 30,000 photographs from around the world. We showcase our work at film festivals, on over 100 public broadcast and cable channels, and on numerous online destinations including explore.org, Snag Films, Hulu and TakePart.

“At explore we are archivists,” explains founder Charles Annenberg Weingarten. “We strive to create films that allow the viewer to join us on our journey as we go on location and experience what unfolds. It’s like the viewers are traveling with the team.”

explore features a wide range of topics—from animal rights, health and human services, and poverty to the environment, education, and spirituality. Delivered in short, digestible bites, explore films appeal to viewers of all ages, from children learning about other cultures for the first time to adults looking for a fresh perspective on the world around them.

explore films include: a woman in Mumbai who has dedicated her life to rescuing young women from prostitution, a priest in Los Angeles who helps gang members reintegrate into society, a project to protect wild mountain gorillas in Rwanda, and an environmental university in Costa Rica that focuses on sustainable development.

Individuals and organizations featured in many explore films also receive explore funding in the form of Annenberg Foundation grants. To date, over $69 million has been awarded to over 300 non-profit organizations worldwide.

explore.org, the online portal for explore, is a community destination where people share thoughts, engage in dialogue, view and email films and photographs, and embed their favorites on blogs and social networking sites.

Never stop learning™. Come explore.

Our Mission
To champion the selfless acts of others, create a portal into the soul of humanity and inspire lifelong learning.

Explore.org is the world's leading philanthropic live nature cam network and documentary film channel.

The Vision of “Shared Safety”

When it comes to safety – on the road, up in the air or in the workplace – there’s simply no room for error. That’s why CheckBAC was created: to provide the promise of “Shared Safety” to communities and workforces through the most accessible and widely adopted platform ever created: their mobile phone.

Courts, businesses, healthcare organizations and government agencies must all play a role in improving treatment options that effectively combat the corrosive effects of alcohol use. Interlock devices and other conventional breathalyzer systems provide a degree of monitoring capabilities, but they’re expensive and limited in their capabilities. There’s never been a system that can monitor individuals in a real time, nearly fool-proof manner… until now.

CheckBAC™ is a patent-pending solution that finally bridges the gap between testing and accountability – so you can easily and effectively monitor and manage the sobriety of hundreds or even thousands of individuals. It combines industry-accurate Bluetooth® technology, a simple, easy-to-use smartphone app and an easy-to-use management platform. CheckBAC gives you the power to test and monitor individuals – anywhere, anytime – and instantly see their results. And it costs up to 80% less than other systems.

Driven by Safety™

CheckBAC is the flagship solution from Driven by Safety™, a technology company headquartered in the metro Atlanta area. Our mission is to deliver the promise of “Shared Safety” to communities, businesses and organizations through mobile applications – and save lives.

Review Text: Julie Hwang, a massage therapist and representative of Aurum Wellness Massage — a small fitness and wellness company located in South Miami, Florida, with between 1 and 10 employees — engaged Web Design Glory in December 2024 to support the business's growth and digital expansion through a comprehensive suite of web and marketing services. The scope of the project was broad and ambitious for a small business, encompassing the full design and development of the company's website, configuration of domain registration and hosting infrastructure, as well as the delivery of search engine optimization (SEO) and social media marketing (SMM) services. Web Design Glory initiated the project with a preliminary website draft that included a basic layout structure, a homepage framework, several internal pages, and some visual design elements. However, the engagement quickly deteriorated from that early stage onward. The client reported that Web Design Glory failed to deliver a completed or professionally finished website at any point during or after the engagement. The site was never published to a live environment, contained visible placeholder content throughout, and exhibited numerous quality control failures including formatting inconsistencies and spelling errors — issues that should have been caught and corrected before any client-facing review. Furthermore, Julie and her team were never granted access to the completed website files or the full source code, leaving the business without any tangible deliverable it could use or transfer to another provider. Beyond the website itself, Web Design Glory also failed to produce any completed SEO strategy, social media campaign, performance analytics, or any other measurable marketing output — meaning that none of the originally agreed-upon project objectives were achieved. From a project management standpoint, the experience was described as inconsistent and disorganized, with deadlines routinely missed and deliverables repeatedly delayed without adequate explanation or proactive communication. All correspondence was conducted via email and messaging applications, and no effective escalation or resolution process was ever established. When asked what stood out positively about the vendor, Julie responded that nothing impressed her — an unusually direct and telling assessment that encapsulated the full failure of the engagement. She noted that Web Design Glory would need to substantially improve its approach to timeline setting, milestone communication, quality assurance standards, and transparency around delays in order to be considered a reliable partner for any future client. The overall engagement was rated 1.5 out of 5.0, with particularly damaging scores of 0.5 for service and deliverables and 1.0 for willingness to refer — reflecting a project that failed on virtually every measurable dimension and left the client without any usable output for their investment of under $10,000.

Additionally, the team contributed beyond development by assisting with presentation materials and refining the product narrative, further supporting the client’s go-to-market efforts. linkedin company about:
With a background in fine arts and an astute business acumen, Hardy established Creative Business after realizing the growing need for financial literacy in the art world. Expanding her focus over the years to work with creative entrepreneurs across all industries, Hardy has since helped countless companies successfully grow and achieve long-term financial sustainability.

About
I believe the future of creative and professional services belongs to firms that prioritize their people and evolve with technology—not get buried by it. I’m here to help leaders navigate that change, tell the truth about what it really takes to grow, and share what I’ve learned along the way.

Twenty years ago, I founded Creative Business Inc. to bring operational clarity, financial rigor, and strategic support to creative entrepreneurs building big, bold businesses. I kept seeing how these founders weren’t being taken seriously when it came to the enormous potential of their businesses. And I was determined to change that.

Today, CBI is a thriving accounting and advisory practice that’s supported hundreds of companies across the creative economy. We've helped our clients grow confidently, navigate chaos, and build businesses that reflect both their values and ambitions.

Along the way, I realized the tools we were using weren’t built for complex firms like mine (and so many of my peers). So I took matters into my own hands and created the tech platform I wish I had all along. Before I knew it, I had stepped into my second founder role, launching Levvy in 2021. 

Levvy is a next-generation practice management platform designed for CAS and outsourced services firms. And just like I envisioned, it grew into the system I always wished I had: one that accounts for the messy, human reality of running a firm—a reality I’ve been living personally for the past two decades.

With Levvy, we’re giving firm owners:

- Clarity on capacity and workflow
- The context people need to move faster and think smarter
- Visibility across work, clients, and outcomes
- Data they can actually use to grow

And we’re building it the way I’ve always worked: collaboratively, transparently, and with real respect for the people doing the work.

If you’re a firm owner who’s curious about Levvy, schedule time with me here: https://calendly.com/jeanne-levvy 

If you’re a creative entrepreneur interested in working with CBI, get in touch here: ​https://creativebusinessinc.com/contact/

Review Text: The client, a proptech company operating a rental marketplace platform, partnered with Venture Aviator to design and develop a comprehensive web-based solution that connects renters and landlords through a fully digital experience. The primary objective was to build a scalable, secure, and feature-rich platform capable of handling complex real-time interactions, including a unique bidding system where users can place and respond to competing rental offers dynamically. Venture Aviator was involved end-to-end, taking ownership of architecture design, technology selection, and full-cycle development. They implemented a robust technical stack including PHP, AngularJS, and React, ensuring the platform could support increasing user demand and future scalability requirements. The solution included critical functionalities such as property search, rental applications, credit report integration, maintenance requests, and real-time auction-style bidding, alongside third-party integrations like Dwolla for payment processing and TeleSign for electronic signatures. A cross-functional team of approximately 15 professionals, including project managers, developers, designers, and QA specialists, collaborated closely throughout the engagement. The results were highly successful, with the platform achieving strong market traction, consistent monthly growth rates, and industry recognition through multiple awards, including the Blockchain Best Industry Application Award. From a project management standpoint, Venture Aviator demonstrated exceptional reliability, consistently meeting all deadlines without delays—a standout quality in complex software projects. Their communication approach was structured and proactive, involving regular weekly updates and iterative feedback loops to ensure alignment with business goals. Additionally, they maintained post-launch support, ensuring system stability and responsiveness to ongoing queries. The client highlighted the vendor’s ability to deliver high-quality output at competitive pricing, striking an effective balance between cost-efficiency and technical excellence. Overall, the engagement resulted in a high-performing, scalable platform that continues to support business growth, backed by a strong partnership characterized by trust, responsiveness, and technical expertise.

Service Outsourced: Custom Software Development / Data Processing Integration
Review Text: Brian Farberov, Vice President of Sales & Marketing at UKAM Industrial Superhard Tools — a specialized small manufacturer of precision diamond cutting tools and machines based in Valencia, Los Angeles, California — engaged Venture Aviator to solve a critical operational inefficiency that had developed over years of using multiple disconnected software systems. As a niche manufacturer serving advanced industries such as microelectronics, semiconductors, aerospace, advanced ceramics, optics, composites, and geology, UKAM required a highly tailored solution that could unify its existing data workflows without forcing a wholesale replacement of the tools its team had already built habits and processes around. The core challenge was that data had to be manually entered across two to three separate systems, creating redundancy, increasing the risk of errors, and consuming significant employee time. Venture Aviator was brought in through a trusted referral — a business school connection of one of the firm's founders — which gave the engagement a high level of trust from the outset, though Brian still due-diligently solicited competing price quotes from other vendors before committing. The scope of work involved Venture Aviator's team embedding themselves in UKAM's day-to-day operations for several days at no charge, carefully observing how data flowed across departments before writing a single line of code. From those observations, they designed and built a centralized data processing platform using C++ and Visual Basic that acted as a unifying layer — allowing UKAM to retain its legacy applications while feeding all data into a single synchronized system that automatically distributed updates across all platforms. Additionally, Venture Aviator diagnosed and resolved a series of pre-existing issues within UKAM's e-commerce platform, which had been experiencing persistent and unresolved problems. The project team consisted of five to six developers, some of whom were based in Ukraine, and the entire collaboration was managed remotely through Google Drive, Google Apps, email, Skype, and phone communication. While the original project scope was quoted at $5,000, the final development cost came to $8,500 — an increase that Brian attributed entirely to UKAM's own decision to add features mid-project, not to scope creep or vendor mismanagement. Deliverables were completed on time, with at most a single day's deviation from milestones. Post-launch, the platform performed reliably with only minor bugs in the early stages, all of which were resolved within a single business day. Brian reported that the platform had saved the company significant time, eliminated the need for duplicate data entry, streamlined employee workflow, and generated zero internal complaints from staff. He rated Venture Aviator a perfect 5.0 across all dimensions — quality, schedule, cost, and willingness to refer — and specifically highlighted the firm's willingness to invest time upfront without billing for observation hours as a key differentiator. Brian concluded that Venture Aviator is particularly well-suited for small businesses attempting to build custom software solutions in a cost-effective and professionally managed manner.
