linkedin company about:
The Scleral Lens Education Society (SLS) was established in 2009 as an organization to help bring professional consensus to the suddenly rapidly growing area of scleral lenses. The mission statement of the SLS reads: “The Scleral Lens Education Society (SLS) is a non-profit organization 501(c)(3) committed to teaching contact lens practitioners the science and art of fitting all designs of scleral contact lenses for the purpose of managing corneal irregularity and ocular surface disease. SLS supports public education that highlights the benefits and availability of scleral contact lenses.”

Beginning with the founding board which included world renown experts in scleral lens fitting such as Greg DeNaeyer, OD, Christine Sindt, OD, and Bruce Baldwin, OD, PhD, the SLS has worked to spread the word about the potential benefits of scleral lens wear to both providers and patients alike. Professional education has included scleral lens webinars, workshops, and lecture series that are always standing room only events.

Currently, the SLS has over 2000 member contact lens practitioners as well as over 50 fellows, or certified scleral lens fitters that have demonstrated their expertise through a peer reviewed process of case reports, publications, and lectures. Many of these members and fellows are international, with SLS fellows from 11 countries, 5 different continents, and 20 different states in the US. Members hail from all 50 states, 6 continents, and over 40 countries.

In addition, the SLS has numerous industry sponsors that support the mission of the society to provide patient access to experienced fitters across the world. The sponsors provide the resources that allow the educational opportunities for practitioners as well as the website and patient resources that are available.

Review Text: The collaboration with Ripe Media was a technical partnership focused on the architecture and deployment of a custom e-learning platform specifically designed to host complex harm reduction training modules. The scope involved converting a high-volume library of in-person workshop materials into a series of interactive, self-paced digital courses compatible with the organization’s existing web infrastructure. Our Founding Story
We met over 10 years ago in New York City working in a direct services harm reduction program. We’ve all thrived doing street-based outreach and care coordination, as well as overseeing a drop-in space, along with dozens of staff, peers, interns, and volunteers. We know how rewarding and exhausting the work can be: we’re reversed dozens of overdoses, had to navigate loss of community members, and keep an organization afloat to meet community needs. Since we worked together under one roof, we’ve all gone on to other opportunities: Taeko has overseen and launched national-level policy, online training courses, capacity building teams, and grant-making initiatives at National Harm Reduction Coalition. Sarah also continued to do organizational development and training as a consultant, worked at the Washington state department of health, and most recently oversaw one of the largest syringe access programs in Seattle. Hector has been working in outpatient treatment, emergency-room based naloxone distribution programs, and clinical services before most recently joining the New York City Department of Health & Mental Hygiene. All three have continued to offer organizational development and leadership coaching on the side. We’ve always shared a passion for supporting programs with systems for leadership development because we remember how hard it was to create them when we were doing direct services. We’re taking what we’ve learned and bringing it together to offer support for organizations who need what we needed ten years ago. Why reinvent the wheel?

About BrightQuery
BrightQuery ("BQ") https://brightquery.com/ is the definitive source of accurate, government-verified data on businesses worldwide. Founded in 2019 and headquartered in Irvine, California, BrightQuery began as a U.S.-focused data provider — building its foundation on official filings from more than 100,000 federal, state, and local government agencies. Over time, BrightQuery has expanded significantly beyond its U.S. origins to become a truly global data platform, now covering 222 countries and tracking 274 million organizations, 226 million legal entities, and 1.2 billion people worldwide. This global expansion reflects BQ’s mission to make the world more factual — one dataset at a time — and positions BQ as the most comprehensive government-verified entity database on the planet. BQ’s data model is built on the concept of the Five Pillars of the Economy — a unified entity framework that mirrors how the real world operates: organizations doing business, legal entities holding ownership, people making decisions, locations operating facilities, and addresses anchoring everything in physical space.

Unlike providers that rely on self-reported information, scraped websites, or investor-facing documents, BQ sources data from official government filings -- including the IRS, U.S. Department of Labor, SEC, Small Business Administration, U.S. Postal Service, and more than 100,000 federal, state, and local agencies -- and enriches it with public web sources where government coverage is limited. The result is a comprehensive, continuously updated database covering over 85 million active and inactive U.S. companies, from Fortune 500 multinationals to sole proprietors, with financial history stretching back to 2017.

linkedin company about:
MyWorkDrive delivers secure, VPN-free access to enterprise file infrastructure without migration. The platform connects existing storage to modern access requirements while preserving infrastructure and permission models.

Storage Connectivity
Connects directly to Windows SMB shares, Azure Files, Azure NetApp Files, Amazon FSx for NetApp, SharePoint, OneDrive, and Azure Blob with Data Lake Gen2. Files remain in existing repositories. NTFS ACLs, SharePoint permissions, and Data Lake access controls continue as configured.

Identity Integration
Deploy using Active Directory with SAML 2.0 SSO or Microsoft Entra ID. Authentication occurs at the identity provider with existing MFA and conditional access policies. Operates on least-privilege principles with no permission elevation capability.

User Experience
Access through web browsers, Windows and macOS mapped drives, and iOS and Android mobile apps. Desktop Office works through mapped drives with traditional file locking. Browser editing uses Office Online with temporary Microsoft 365 staging and automatic write-back.

Security and Governance
All connections use TLS 1.2 or higher. Optional DLP controls include download blocking, watermarking, and clipboard restrictions per share or group. Device approval governs client access. Audit logging exports to SIEM via Syslog.
Supports compliance efforts for HIPAA, FINRA, GDPR, and CMMC by preserving control of identity, data location, and audit. SOC 2 Type II certified.

Deployment
Ranges from single-server implementations to multi-node clusters behind load balancers. Publishing options include direct HTTPS, reverse proxy, Microsoft Entra Application Proxy, or Cloud Web Connector with outbound-only connections. Implementation typically completes within days to weeks.
Used by healthcare systems, financial services, legal practices, educational institutions, and manufacturing companies requiring secure file access across distributed teams while maintaining data sovereignty.

Review Text: Brent McNey, CEO of Brenco Solutions, commissioned the development of a comprehensive custom software ecosystem to replace inefficient manual workflows in his nationwide general contracting business. The project, led by the firm's IT Director, involved the creation of an iOS and Android mobile application integrated with a central CMS web portal. The primary objective was to eliminate the technical drawbacks of email by providing commercial and residential clients with real-time photographic documentation and service updates. Mobomo utilized an agile development methodology, delivering a functional prototype in just six weeks, which allowed the team to maintain a working model while iteratively adding complex features like data analytics for profit margins and employee response times. The resulting system significantly improved internal productivity and client transparency, allowing stakeholders to monitor job-related information and service status directly through the portal. This digital transformation has standardized Brenco's data collection processes and established a scalable foundation for their nationwide operations.

The Rise of Experiential Retail Stores
This article explores the growing trend of experiential retail stores that prioritize memorable customer experiences over traditional transactional shopping, examining design strategies, technology integration, and business models transforming physical retail into destination experiences.


Experiential retail represents a fundamental shift in how physical stores create value as e-commerce handles routine purchasing efficiently. Rather than competing purely on price and convenience, forward-thinking retailers transform stores into immersive brand experiences that engage customers emotionally and socially. These spaces blend shopping with entertainment, education, and community building through interactive displays, workshops, cafes, and Instagram-worthy environments. Experiential stores recognize that physical locations must offer something digital channels cannot replicate, creating reasons for customers to visit beyond immediate purchase needs. This trend spans categories from cosmetics and apparel to technology and home goods as retailers reimagine store purposes for the omnichannel era, fostering deeper connections and brand loyalty.

Successful experiential retail design prioritizes engagement over efficiency. Stores incorporate hands-on product testing areas where customers experiment with merchandise before buying, from cooking demonstrations with kitchen tools to virtual reality experiences showcasing travel destinations. Beauty retailers offer complimentary consultations and makeovers that educate while driving product sales. Bookstores host author events and reading clubs, building literary communities. Technology stores provide genius bars and training sessions that support products throughout ownership. These interactive elements extend dwell time, create social media moments customers share organically, and build emotional connections that pure e-commerce transactions cannot match. Such innovative designs are crucial in transforming stores into vital community hubs that foster ongoing customer relationships.

Technology enables experiential retail without sacrificing operational efficiency. Augmented reality mirrors allow virtual product trials without physical inventory. Interactive displays provide detailed product information and customization options that would require extensive staff time. Mobile checkout systems eliminate traditional register lines, allowing staff to focus on consultation and assistance. Digital integration connects in-store experiences with customer profiles and purchase histories to enable personalization. Smart mirrors suggest complementary products and alternative sizes, enhancing the shopping experience. These technologies create seamless experiences blending physical and digital advantages while collecting valuable data about customer preferences and behaviors that inform merchandising and marketing strategies, driving innovation in retail.

Business models evolve as experiential retail redefines store economics. Traditional metrics like sales per square foot become less relevant when space serves multiple purposes including brand building, content creation, customer education, and community engagement. Some retailers operate experiential flagships at losses justified by brand awareness and data collection supporting broader omnichannel operations. Others monetize experiences directly through paid workshops, membership programs, or premium services. Stores become showrooms supporting online purchasing with in-store pickup and returns. Successful experiential retailers measure success through customer lifetime value, social media engagement, and brand health metrics alongside traditional sales figures. As consumer expectations evolve, experiential retail shifts from trend to necessity for physical stores justifying their continued relevance.

Understanding the consumer psychology behind experiential retail reveals why such models succeed in creating lasting brand impressions. By engaging multiple senses, retailers can elicit emotional responses that resonate with customers long after they leave the store. This multisensory approach not only creates memorable shopping experiences but also strengthens emotional connections, influencing purchase decisions and fostering brand advocacy. Iconic brands like Apple have capitalized on these strategies, designing stores that are not just points of sale but spaces for education, creativity, and connection, setting benchmarks for others in the industry. As the landscape continues to evolve, the integration of psychology into retail design will be essential for creating environments that attract and retain customers.

Experiential retail's impact is visible in its ability to transform traditional shopping malls into vibrant community spaces. Retail developers are increasingly integrating leisure and entertainment components, like theaters, art installations, and events, that draw visitors beyond shopping. This shift changes the mall from a collection of individual stores to a cohesive experience that encourages longer visits and repeat business. Cities worldwide are investing in such developments, understanding that successful retail environments stimulate local economies by attracting both locals and tourists. This holistic approach to mall design emphasizes experiential elements, making malls a central social venue rather than just a shopping destination.

The rise of pop-up stores within the experiential retail framework showcases innovation and flexibility. Temporary retail spaces allow brands to test new concepts, engage with different demographics, and create exclusive experiences that generate buzz. These pop-ups can transform urban landscapes by occupying unexpected locations, from old warehouses to public parks, altering the consumer's perception of shopping areas. The success of these ventures often lies in their limited availability, creating a sense of urgency and exclusivity that appeals to the modern consumer. When well-executed, pop-up stores contribute to the dynamic evolution of experiential retail, offering brands a platform to explore creativity and expand their reach.

Sustainability becomes an integral consideration in the evolution of experiential retail. Today's consumers are increasingly eco-conscious, seeking brands that demonstrate environmental responsibility. Experiential retail environments can reflect these values through eco-friendly designs, sustainable materials, and processes that minimize waste. Retailers implementing green initiatives, such as recycling programs or energy-efficient technologies, attract socially responsible consumers, adding another layer of engagement. As environmental concerns continue to influence purchasing decisions, integrating sustainability into experiential retail practices not only aligns with consumer values but also enhances brand image and loyalty.

The future of experiential retail is poised for further transformation through innovative technologies and evolving consumer expectations. Retailers are beginning to explore artificial intelligence and machine learning to predict consumer needs and personalize in-store experiences even further. Virtual and augmented reality could redefine the boundaries of immersive shopping, creating hyper-personalized environments that adapt in real-time. Additionally, the fusion of experiential retail with other industries, such as hospitality and entertainment, could lead to a new era of multi-sensory experiences. As these trends continue to grow, experiential retail will become a crucial differentiator in attracting consumers in a competitive landscape.

Where Sports Meets Showbiz: 2025 Trends
The sports and entertainment worlds converge across streaming, cinemas, live events, and social media. From sports docuseries and athlete-hosted podcasts to sports movie premieres and athlete cameos, these trends are reshaping fan engagement and marketing.


The line between sports and entertainment is thinner than ever, as streaming platforms, social feeds, and live stages remix athletic narratives for mass audiences. From bingeable sports docuseries that peel back the curtain on training camps to athlete-hosted podcasts that deliver unfiltered conversations, fans are getting access that once belonged only to insiders. The result is a year-round storyline that keeps teams relevant long after the final whistle. Sports networks are also investing in original storytelling, producing docu-series that rival Hollywood's best to captivate audiences with behind-the-scenes drama. Beyond the entertainment aspect, these docuseries stimulate discussions about athletes' life choices and career paths, encouraging viewer engagement through social media debates and forums.

On the big screen and the red carpet, studios are timing sports movie premieres to coincide with marquee games, turning televised matchups into full-week cultural events. Casting directors are also leaning into athlete cameos that bring authenticity—and extra buzz—to films and prestige TV, while sports celebrity gossip drives the meme economy between releases. The playbook is clear: align narratives, star power, and calendar moments to maximize reach. Films based on historical sporting events are becoming popular, drawing in viewers who appreciate the blend of fact and fiction. Additionally, exclusive red-carpet events for these releases attract a diverse range of fans and media. This strategic alignment not only boosts viewership but also strengthens the emotional connection fans have with both the film and the sport.

The live experience is evolving too, with esports arena events adopting the spectacle of championship weekends and courting traditional athletes as guests and investors. These crossovers amplify brand partnerships, unlock new demographics, and encourage second-screen engagement, from interactive trivia to merch drops. For venues and promoters, the goal is to build hybrid festivals that cater to superfans across gaming and traditional sports. Interactive elements like fan votes and augmented reality experiences are becoming standard, offering attendees a more engaging and participatory presence. These innovations help bridge the gap between different fan bases, creating a unified community that thrives on engagement and shared passions.

Comedy and music round out the package, as writers churn out sports comedy sketches for late-night and streaming variety hours, and labels coordinate sports soundtrack releases that double as marketing for films and playoff runs. Expect more transmedia storytelling that threads clips, behind-the-scenes reels, and fan challenges across platforms, with creators and teams collaborating like showrunners. If you want to stay ahead of the curve, follow the content pipelines where athletes, studios, and platforms meet. This collaborative approach is fostering a new era of creativity, where diverse voices add layers of richness to sports narratives through comedy and music. It's an evolving journey that invites audiences to connect with the creative world behind their favorite sports moments.

Virtual reality is becoming a significant trend where sports intersect with showbiz. VR experiences allow fans to immerse themselves in a game's atmosphere, providing a 360-degree view of the action from the comfort of their homes. These experiences are coupled with interactive elements, such as choosing camera angles or accessing real-time statistics, further blurring the lines between passive viewing and active participation. Platforms like Oculus and PlayStation VR are racing to secure exclusive sporting events to attract tech-savvy audiences. This technology not only enhances the viewers’ connection with their favorite teams but also opens new avenues for advertising and sponsorship deals, making VR a vital frontier in the sports entertainment landscape. Industry analysts predict a growing demand for personalized VR content, with fans eagerly embracing these innovations. The unique storytelling potential within VR could soon become a pivotal way to experience sports dramas and documentaries firsthand.

With the rise of social media influencers, there is a growing trend of collaborations between athletes and online personalities. Brands leverage these connections to tap into diverse audiences, executing marketing campaigns that resonate with the followers of both sports stars and digital creators. This synergy is often seen in the form of live events, such as co-hosted streams or public appearances, where influencers bring their charisma and reach while athletes contribute authenticity and fan loyalty. As a result, marketing strategies are being redefined, with brands focusing on creating narrative-driven content that appeals to a wide spectrum of viewers, bridging the sports and entertainment sectors even further. Successful campaigns highlight the personal stories of athletes and influencers, bringing a relatable and humanizing element to marketing efforts. This method transforms advertisements from mere promotions to engaging narratives that capture viewer imaginations.

The fusion of sports and showbiz is further energized by cutting-edge technology such as artificial intelligence (AI) and machine learning. Networks and production companies are utilizing AI to analyze audience preferences, leading to tailored content that aligns with fan demands. This approach not only boosts viewership but also enhances fan interaction through personalized recommendations, interactive fan polls, and live-chat features during broadcasts. Furthermore, AI-driven cameras capture highlights and angles previously unattainable in traditional settings, adding a new dimension to sports entertainment. As the industry continues to innovate, AI's role in analyzing and enhancing content could redefine how audiences engage with both sports and entertainment. This technology extends far beyond viewership, influencing live game strategies, player analytics, and predictive modeling for future sports trends.

Athlete-driven philanthropy and social causes are another intersection where sports and showbiz meet. As public figures, athletes are increasingly using their platforms to advocate for change and support charitable endeavors, often partnering with celebrities and influencers to amplify their impact. These initiatives are widely covered across various media, drawing attention from diverse demographics and fostering a shared sense of community among fans. Partnerships with production companies enable the creation of compelling narratives around these causes, illustrating the power of sports figures to inspire and motivate social progress. As these movements grow, they not only contribute to societal change but also shape the evolving landscape of sports entertainment, where values and visibility go hand in hand. Sports narratives are becoming more than just a game, evolving into platforms for storytelling that challenge and inspire global audiences.

Areas of Research

Traumatic Brain Injury (TBI)
Traumatic Brain Injury (TBI)
Traumatic Brain Injury occurs when a forceful blow or jolt to the head disrupts the normal function of the brain. Symptoms of TBI can vary widely and can be subtle, and there's no single definitive test for it, making diagnosis challenging. Blinkcns' EyeStat technology offers an objective and accurate assessment of brain function through the blink reflex, providing researchers and doctors insights into neurological activity following a head injury.

Learn More

Parkinson's Disease
Parkinson's Disease
Parkinson's Disease is a chronic neurodegenerative disorder that affects movement. Diagnosing it typically takes an average of 4-6 years due to the lack of a definitive test and the subjectivity of symptom evaluation, leading to potential misdiagnosis, especially in the early stages.

Leveraging the blink reflex technology, blinkcns collaborates with pharmaceutical and academic partners to improve diagnosis and clinical trials and empower patients with remote monitoring tools.

Review Text: Jennifer Smith, Executive Director of the South Texas Alliance for Orphans, shared a highly successful experience regarding the digital transformation of the organization's Babysitting Collaborative program. The project addressed a critical bottleneck: a manual, time-consuming application process for prospective babysitters and foster agencies that involved sensitive personal information. Kinetech Cloud developed a secure, mobile-optimized online application and backend system that streamlined the entire workflow. Key features included electronic document signing, the ability to upload photo documentation, and a task-based checklist that allows users to save and resume progress. By converting manual paperwork into auto-populating online forms, the solution created a secure environment for all parties to access necessary files and generated complete digital packets for agencies. Jennifer highlighted the efficiency of the development rhythm, which utilized bi-weekly Zoom meetings to implement feedback incrementally. This strategic investment, ranging from $50,000 to $199,999, not only improved data security and user experience but also significantly increased the operational capacity of the Alliance team to serve the foster care community in San Antonio.
linkedin company about:
The South Texas Alliance for Orphans mobilizes individuals, churches, and businesses to respond to the foster care crisis—together. No one church, person, or family can meet every need. It takes an Alliance of people playing their part so children are supported, families are strengthened, and youth aging out of foster care aren’t left to navigate life alone.

We help people identify their role and step into it with clarity. Through prevention, protection, and provision, we equip communities across Bexar County and Region 8 to serve families impacted by the foster care system in meaningful, sustainable ways.

So far, we’ve helped 78 churches and 4,800+ individuals engage intentionally—through training, collaboration, and practical pathways that make real impact possible.

Our Mission: To serve and equip the Church, individuals, families, and businesses in fulfilling the biblical call to care for children and families impacted by the foster care system.

Our Vision: Every child is part of a safe and thriving family.

Transforming the Foster Care Crisis in San Antonio & Surrounding Areas
Every Relationship Matters.
Many people care deeply about children in foster care but feel unsure where they belong. That tension is real. We help individuals discover their role in the foster care crisis by strengthening the relationships that help children and families thrive.

Get Connected


SOUTH TEXAS ALLIANCE FOR ORPHANS
Built on Relationships That Last
For more than 10 years, the Alliance has worked alongside churches, businesses, families, and the foster care system across South Texas.

136 churches across Region 8 have chosen ongoing relational engagement.

Over time, a clear pattern has emerged:

Churches move from initial interest to sustained relational care.
Biological families receive support before removal becomes necessary.
Foster families are more likely to continue when they are not serving alone.
Relationships outlast programs.
This work is not built on quick outcomes. It is built on relationships that last.

What began as helping a woman repair her car became something more.

Practical support opened the door to ongoing connection.

Needs were met, but more importantly, she was no longer alone.
ABOUT THE FOSTER CRISIS
The Foster Care Crisis Is a Relationship Crisis
Foster care is often the moment a crisis becomes visible. The breakdown usually begins long before a child is removed.

In San Antonio and surrounding areas, too many families reach a breaking point without consistent relationships strong enough to help hold things together.

When support is missing:

Families fracture instead of stabilizing.
Foster and kinship caregivers carry the weight alone and many stop fostering within the first year.
Young adults age out without lasting relationships that protect, guide, and open doors over time.
This is not only a system problem. It is what happens when people are isolated.
See the Full Picture

Not everyone will do the same thing, but everyone can do something.
The Foster Care Crisis as a River
The Foster Care Crisis as a River
The foster care crisis is like a river.

Upstream, families are under pressure. Without support, cracks widen.
Midstream, children enter foster care and caregivers try to keep everyone afloat.

Downstream, young adults face adulthood alone, still navigating the current.

Each part is connected. What happens upstream affects what happens downstream. There are three places people can show up and every one of them matters:


Before Crisis
See the foster care crisis, explore the opportunities to serve

During Foster Care
Surround foster and adoptive families with consistent, relational support.

Aging Out
Walk with young adults for the long haul because stability takes time, connection, and trust.

Alliance Foster Care Crisis Bexar County Every Relationship Matters 61
Where Most People Begin
You Do Not Have to Figure This Out Alone
If you care deeply about children and families impacted by foster care but feel unsure where you fit, you are not alone.

Many of us began in that same place. Aware. Moved. Unsure what faithfulness required.

We cared. We hesitated. We did not want to step into something unsustainable or unsupported.

Over time, we learned that the only options were not foster or adopt. The foster care crisis is a river with more than one place to stand, and every place matters.

We are not here to recruit you into a program. We are here to walk with you as you discern where your presence can be steady, supported, and lasting.

Discover Your Role
Talk With Our Team
A Way Forward
How This Journey Often Unfolds
Engaging the foster care crisis is not about rushing into action. It is about moving from clarity to discernment to supported presence.

Connect with us
1. See Clearly
Understand the foster care crisis as a connected, relational system. See where upstream and downstream pressures shape families and youth.

2. Discern Honestly
Acknowledge your questions and hesitations. Discover where you belong and what you can sustain.

3. Stay Relationally
Step into a supported role and remain open-handed. Commit to relationships that last. Every relationship matters.

What Happens When You Stay
What Staying Shapes in You
Staying close to the foster care crisis will not make you a hero. It will make you present.


Support When the Cost Rises
You receive guidance, support, and pathways that fit your capacity.

This work does not promise quick results or easy answers. But it does anchor you in relationships that last.

A Family Who Stayed
One family began by offering practical support to another household in crisis. They expected a short season of help. Instead, they found themselves walking alongside that family longer than they imagined.

There were setbacks. There were hard conversations. There were moments when leaving would have been easier.

They stayed.

Years later, what remains is not a success story. It is a relationship built on trust. And a faith that was reshaped by proximity.

Alliance Team 2025 Foster Care Crisis Nonprofit San Antonio 29
Led by Those Who Walk It Daily
The People Behind This Work
This work is not led from a distance.

It is carried by people who have stepped into the foster care crisis and chosen to remain. Foster parents, mentors, pastors, advocates. People who have lived the tension, the cost, and the questions that come with staying.

We are not here because we studied it. We are here because we have walked it.

Every relationship matters, and we have seen what that requires over time.

The Foster Care Crisis as a River
A Message from Jennifer Smith
I have spent the last two decades walking alongside children, families, and the people who choose to stay with them.
I have seen what happens when support is present. And I have seen what happens when it is not.
The foster care crisis is not just about systems. It is about relationships.

It is about whether children and families have people close enough to know them, support them, and remain when things become difficult.

This work does not require heroes. It requires people who are willing to stay.

You do not have to do everything. You do not have to have it all figured out.

But once you begin to see clearly, it becomes harder to look away.
My invitation is simple.

Pay attention to what is stirring in you.

Listen for where you are being led.
And consider what it might look like to remain present in one place over time.

Every relationship matters.

These are not polished narratives or quick resolutions. They are honest accounts of what it looks like to remain present when the path is unclear.

Each episode centers on one person and one moment that changed how they saw the crisis and their place within it.

There is a crucial distinction between controlling access to an action and the action controlling itself. Sentry, an "action approval" service, ensures real-time approval by the application before any potentially harmful actions are executed.

Unlike external authentication and IAM applications, Sentry is uniquely integrated within the application, providing a robust defense against potential manipulation and enabling “multi-factor authorization” from real individuals or groups. This innovative approach empowers applications to implement "action approval" for diverse actions, ensuring comprehensive control. Notably, Sentry allows customizable response time periods for each embedded action and authorization request, enhancing security protocols.

With cyber-attacks on the rise, it's vital to have extremely robust security measures in place. Providing total control and peace of mind, these patented services are essential in safeguarding organizations and mankind against threats from various sectorsSpecialties
Identity Usage Management and Application Action Authorization

Real cyber-security is more than just protection against cyber-attacks by putting up firewalls and controlling access. It is fully controlling the actions within activities which can have devasting impacts by requiring approval for them to continue, regardless if they were accessed & started somehow. In today's AI world, the embedment of HI into systems is becoming especially important.

Get Approvals Now

You can contact any individual with Sentry, the personal cyber-security guard, in their lives to obtain approval for any action to continue to which they are associated. Overview

Real cyber-security consists of Authentication, Authorization, Approval & Accounting.

Authentication is “Verify an Identity” while Authorization is “Give Permissions”, Approval is “Make a Decision” and Accounting is "Record Events". Today, together, Authentication and Authorization control access of an identity to an application with its activities consisting of actions with Accounting recording the cyber-security event details.

What is missing is "Approval" which requires a second “positive” decision from an approver to be made before an activity or an action proceeds to run. There is a big difference between controlling access to an action vs the action controlling itself. Sentry is an "action approval" service that empowers an application to obtain real-time approval before any initiated actions with possible negative impacts can continue execution. Real-time approval from a specified individual or group.

Unlike authentication and IAM applications which are external to the application's actions, Sentry services are added as very small modifications of the application actions themselves preventing possible manipulation. Authentication and IAM applications, even if multi-factored, only control access to an application. Sentry enables an application to confirm an action’s continuation from one or more real people, i.e. “Multi-Factor Approval”. Applications can be updated with “action approval” of multiple, different actions targeting various individuals or groups for absolute control. The required approval response time periods are definable for each action and each action approval request.

With the number of cyber-attacks, both criminal & terrorist, continuing to grow and targeting individuals, businesses and governments from every demographic, having a service that can give complete & secure control of any organization’s processes is absolutely vital. Consent PreferencesAbout Us
Who is Suntell?
Our Story
Innovation Born from Experience. Driven by What’s Next.
Suntell was founded in 1996 by commercial lenders who saw firsthand how outdated, manual processes were slowing down lending teams. From duplicative data entry to disconnected systems and endless checklists, managing a loan portfolio was far too complex—and far from strategic.

They knew there had to be a smarter way.

So they built it.

Suntell pioneered the first Loan Management System (LMS) designed to streamline commercial and agricultural lending from start to finish. But we didn’t stop there. We listened to our customers, anticipated the industry’s evolution, and continued to build.

We added tools like:

Suntell Document Imaging (SDI) to centralize borrower, loan, and deposit documentation,
AgExam for in-depth agricultural analysis,
Stress Testing to proactively assess risk, and
Loan Queue & Dashboards to streamline workflows and boost team performance.
And we didn’t stop there.

These additions—and many more—have evolved into the Square 1 Credit Suite®, an innovative, ever-advancing platform that continues to grow with the needs of our customers and the changing demands of the industry.


Our Mission
Our mission is to empower community lending institutions to build strong, scalable, and compliant commercial and agricultural lending programs—through one powerful, integrated solution.

Suntell
Our Leadership Team

Veronica “Ronnie” Wooten
CEO
Ronnie joined Suntell two years after its launch and played an integral role in building the original foundation and culture upon which we operate today. An efficiency and process expert, Ronnie conceived the organization’s departmental and reporting structure in a way that allows input and feedback from our customers to be heard and help drive our products’ development and advancement in the marketplace. A champion for work/life balance, Ronnie has cultivated a creative approach to achieving results while delighting our customers which has facilitated a strong, tenured employee base with low turnover rates. This, in turn, allows our company to maintain low overhead costs while maximizing our customers’ satisfaction rates.


Hugh Khan
Chief Technology Officer
Hugh Khan joined Suntell as the Chief Technology Officer (CTO), bringing with him an impressive record of pioneering software development. Hugh’s deep industry insights have created growth at fintech startups and transformed e-commerce operations through innovative strategies. With an eye for emerging AI technologies and agile methodologies, he has shaped pivotal change in both technical and strategic realms. Hugh is dedicated to fostering strong interpersonal relationships within his team, emphasizing collaboration and focusing a client-centric approach to achieving goals. He embodies Suntell’s vision to empower community banking with groundbreaking digital solutions.


Kerry Ronquillo

A seasoned business development professional, Kerry joined Suntell in 2003. His keen listening skills and tenure with our company and products afford him the opportunity to uncover the real pain points and offer real solutions.

His product knowledge and ability to quickly understand pain points and identify solutions make him an invaluable resource to our team and our customers. A tireless advocate for our customers, he recognizes the importance of their success and consistently goes above and beyond to ensure their expectations are met or exceeded. Ryan’s tenacity and dedication to delighting our customers and fostering the company’s culture are exuded throughout his entire team of service professionals.

Reach-out Message: Hi Jim, Leading the Handicapped Scuba Association while building accessible training systems reflects a powerful mission-driven approach. How do you ensure complex certification workflows stay intuitive for users across such diverse needs? Looking forward to connecting. Parag Google | Founder SoftwareBrio.com
Review Text: Design Genious Corp. partnered with the Handicapped Scuba Association (HSA) to modernize and rebuild a highly complex web platform that supports global scuba training and certification programs for individuals with disabilities. The engagement began with a critical need to update outdated PHP code that had been developed over several years and was at risk of becoming obsolete, potentially disrupting backend operations. The team successfully upgraded the legacy codebase, ensuring continuity and stability of the system while resolving key functional issues, particularly within the login and user management areas. Following this, they undertook the development of a new, interactive website designed to handle a wide range of advanced functionalities, including certification tracking across multiple levels—from snorkelers to instructor trainers—as well as management of dive buddies, instructors, and training programs. The platform is designed to accommodate diverse user needs, supporting individuals with various physical and cognitive disabilities, making both frontend usability and backend logic highly complex. The project has been executed by a dedicated development team led by a project manager who maintained near-daily communication with the client, ensuring alignment and responsiveness throughout the engagement. The collaboration has been highly efficient, with the team consistently solving intricate technical challenges and delivering steady progress on the new system. The updated backend has significantly improved functionality, while the new website, nearing launch, offers a more modern interface and smoother operations. The client highlighted the team’s exceptional communication, problem-solving capabilities, and commitment to understanding the organization’s mission as key strengths. Overall, the partnership resulted in a robust, scalable, and mission-critical platform that empowers HSA to continue delivering life-changing training programs worldwide while maintaining operational reliability and long-term digital growth.

Reviewer Company: Association for the Advancement of Wound Care
Review Text: RocketBuild partnered with the Association for the Advancement of Wound Care to complete a web-based integration between the organization’s association management system and a third-party scientific literature database. The association needed to provide its members with seamless one-click access to Trip Database Pro as a members-only benefit, but its existing AMS, MemberClicks, lacked the sophistication required to support single sign-on and membership authentication on its own. RocketBuild was brought in to assess the technical limitations, coordinate with the AMS provider and the external database team, and build a functional bridge that allowed authenticated members to access the research database directly from the association’s website. Despite unexpected technical constraints and scope complications related to the AMS, RocketBuild remained persistent and solution-oriented, completing the integration successfully. The final outcome gave AAWC members a smoother digital experience and improved access to valuable scientific literature resources without requiring complicated manual steps. Communication was handled primarily through Connor Hess, who provided regular progress updates, maintained professional coordination with all stakeholders, and managed collaboration effectively despite time-zone differences with the third-party database team. The client highlighted RocketBuild’s responsiveness, flexibility, professionalism, and willingness to work within a nonprofit budget as major strengths. Although the cost was higher than what the organization would normally afford, the team showed flexibility and tenacity, delivering a reliable integration that strengthened the association’s member value proposition and supported its broader mission of advancing wound care education and professional access.

The engagement began with in-depth planning sessions where the client collaborated directly with the founder and technical team to define the software’s structure, functionality, and long-term vision. The development was executed in multiple structured phases, starting with architecture design, followed by feature development, real-time testing in field conditions, and iterative improvements based on continuous feedback. The platform enabled inspectors to conduct inspections, upload reports directly to the cloud, and allowed clients to log in and access real-time data about their properties, inspections, and maintenance activities. This significantly improved communication, transparency, and operational efficiency across the business. One of the most impactful outcomes was the ability for both clients and inspectors to provide direct feedback within the system, enabling faster iterations and continuous product improvement. The workflow efficiency increased substantially, and the software became a key differentiator for ParkPro Tek, helping them expand partnerships with other playground companies. Flint Hills Group demonstrated exceptional project management through consistent communication via Slack, Zoom, and weekly scrum calls, ensuring alignment at every stage. Their responsiveness stood out, as they often resolved critical issues in real-time—even while field teams were actively using the system. The client highlighted their flexibility, scalability, and ability to go beyond initial requirements by proactively enhancing the software without additional costs. Overall, the partnership resulted in a highly effective, user-friendly platform that transformed ParkPro Tek’s operations and positioned them for long-term growth in the industry.

Review Text: Sunrise Integration partnered with XanderPay, a travel technology and financial services company, to develop custom API solutions that significantly improved their payment processing capabilities. The engagement focused on building two core systems: a batch processing payment API and a hotel banking enrollment platform. The payment API was designed to extract key financial data from weekly reports, consolidate it into structured formats, and automate payouts to hotels via a partner system. This drastically reduced manual processing time—from hours to minutes—allowing the client to scale operations efficiently, even when dealing with thousands of hotel transactions. The second solution involved creating a multi-step enrollment form that streamlined how hotels submitted banking information, replacing manual communication with automated data capture and processing. Throughout the engagement, Sunrise Integration demonstrated strong adaptability, especially as project requirements evolved and expanded. Despite multiple reassessments, shifting timelines, and increased scope, the team consistently delivered functional and reliable solutions. Their project management approach, driven through Zoom meetings and email communication, ensured continuity and progress even under changing conditions. The client highlighted Sunrise Integration’s exceptional work ethic, responsiveness, and technical capability, noting that the APIs performed exactly as required and enabled major operational efficiencies. Overall, the partnership resulted in scalable, automated systems that improved speed, accuracy, and overall payment workflow for XanderPay’s global operations.

Review Text: John Chatmas is the CEO of Waterloo Capital, a financial services firm focused on sophisticated public and private market investment capabilities, family office services, and high-level financial planning. In the referenced project, Waterloo Capital worked with Touch To on software development, UX/UI design, and web development to improve and modernize its platform. The engagement included presenting new redesign options, creating a new scoring model updated through an API, building a new backend login and admin portal, and reworking much of the existing codebase so updates could function correctly. Touch To also worked on fixing broken macroeconomic statistics, improving error logging through Sentry.io, adding timestamp fields for API activity, resolving issues with metrics that were not updating, and supporting portfolio-related API needs. The scope further included Django admin setup, user management capabilities, ticker symbol updates, security admin pages, database and server backup configuration, local development improvements, staging site preparation, and early SaaS roadmap readiness. The team also performed design discovery work, including a website audit, stakeholder interviews, conceptual mockups, and estimates for future design work. Waterloo Capital reported that Touch To fulfilled the entire scope of work successfully and delivered strong project management through structured two-week work blocks. The team communicated clearly through virtual meetings, email, and messaging apps, consistently asked for clarification when needed, and took time to help the client understand the process. Their communication, creativity, listening ability, and detailed note-taking stood out as especially impressive. Overall, the engagement was rated highly across quality, schedule, cost, and willingness to refer, with no areas for improvement identified. :contentReference[oaicite:0]{index=0}

Review Text: Alison Yates, CEO and Co-Founder of Knitrino, engaged Founders Approach to rescue and rebuild a mobile innovation platform for interactive knitting patterns. Following a detailed code audit of previous work, the team developed a custom mobile application and a comprehensive administrative backend for pattern management. The project, which involves a US-based management team and an offshore development squad, utilized an integrated Trello workflow to maintain transparency across global time zones. Since December 2019, the engagement has involved an investment of approximately $70,000 to $199,999, focusing on turning zero-code prototypes into a high-fidelity MVP. The partnership was notably solidified by an in-person intensive kick-off in Baltimore to align on product strategy and communication styles, successfully bridging the gap between complex engineering software principles and specialized consumer hobbyist needs.
linkedin company about:
Knitrino believes you deserve more from your knitting patterns. Knitrino's friendly patterns create a world-class knitting experience centered on uplifting our connected community. Founded by knitting sisters Andrea Cull & Alison Yates, it's like Google Maps for knitting patterns: Click a stitch to see how to do it, highlight your current place, step by step pattern tracking, colorwork in your yarn colors, see only your size, and pin a note anywhere. Our goal is to make knitting more accessible, and to help people grow into the knitters the aspire to be! 

An experienced executive and wearer of many hats, I am currently looking for board positions at companies where I can lend my skills and experience. 

I am co-founder and CEO of Quarky Labs, makers of an innovative mobile app for knitters. Prior to Quarky Labs, I served on the executive leadership team at Zemax, an engineering software company where I lead the product management, user experience and customer support teams. I exited Zemax following the successful sale of the business in 2018. I have a BS and MS in physics and an executive MBA from UW’s Foster School of Business, and over 25 years of business and optics/lasers experience. I love helping other startups with customer research, go-to-market strategies, and product-market fit. When I’m not frantically building products, I can be found biking long distances or playing the piano.

Your Smile, Your Story
At Lund Orthodontics, we believe your smile is more than just an expression—it’s a reflection of your journey, your confidence, and your well-being. From Invisalign® and clear braces to whitening treatments, we offer modern, compassionate care that meets you where you are. Our award-winning team is here to help you shine.

Why Your Smile Matters
A radiant, healthy smile doesn’t just turn heads—it can transform your life. Orthodontic treatment is an investment in your long-term physical, oral, and emotional wellness. Proper alignment enhances function, reduces stress on your teeth and jaw, and contributes to your overall quality of life.

Here’s how misalignment can impact your daily health:
Uneven teeth may wear down over time
Bite imbalances can strain gum and bone tissue
Jaw alignment issues may lead to speech difficulties
Crowded teeth can increase plaque buildup and risk of gum disease
Improper bite can affect digestion and chewing
Misalignment may contribute to jaw pain, TMJ disorders, and chronic headaches
Overbites can increase the risk of tooth fractures
Tailored Treatment, Just For You
We treat patients of all ages with the same attentive, personalized care. From your very first consultation, we take time to understand your needs, goals, and lifestyle. Whether you’re a busy parent, a working professional, or a student, we offer solutions that fit your schedule—and your budget.

Every treatment plan is designed with intention, using advanced technology and years of clinical expertise to create a seamless, stress-free experience.



More Than Orthodontics—A Partner In Your Wellness
We believe in treating the whole person, not just their teeth. That’s why our approach goes beyond aesthetics to support your total health and happiness. Our care is collaborative, working with dental and medical professionals when needed to ensure you get the most complete care possible.

With flexible financing options, digital forms to streamline your check-in, and a team that treats you like family—we’re here to support you every step of the way.

Ready To Begin?
We can’t wait to welcome you to the Lund Orthodontics family. Come discover what personalized, innovative orthodontic care can do for your life. Start your smile transformation today.

Let’s begin your story—schedule your consultation now.

What It Means to be FDIC-Insured

Being FDIC-insured is a big deal. The Federal Deposit Insurance Corporation (FDIC) is an independent agency of the United States government that protects the funds depositors place in banks and savings associations. ABOUT US

Meet The Country Carpenters Team

From humble beginnings in Bolton, Connecticut and with a passionate desire to preserve the cultural and structural integrity that our New England ancestors adhered to, Roger Barrett Sr. established Country Carpenters in 1974 along with his wife Lois and a team of dedicated, skilled crafstmen.

Although fifty years and three generations have come to pass, our mission and philosophy at Country Carpenters remains the same. Since our first building went up in 1974, we have been helping loyal customers across the country fulfill their dreams of owning beautiful, period style homes and post and beam buildings through the success of our unique outdoor building kits.

As we celebrate our 50th anniversary, we’re forging our way into the future with an effort to better clarify the family of brands our team has provided now for three generations. In doing so, we are excited to expand our capabilities by establishing a family of brands under the Country Carpenters name. Early New England Homes by Country Carpenters, and Post & Beam Barns by Country Carpenters now operate under the same family company, with the same values, vision, and philosophy that has satisfied customers for over 50 years. We plan to continue exceeding our clients’ expectations by creating quality handcrafted barn-style garages, country cabins, sheds, storage buildings, garden building kits, & more for the next 50 years.

A CALLING not to forget our rich COLONIAL HERITAGE.
Wanting the FLAVOR OF LIFE once found in the SIMPLICITY OF THE PAST. Providing a building that is in HARMONY with the COUNTRYSIDE we live in. A CONCERN TO RESTORE FAMILY VALUES at HOME AND IN THE COMMUNITY
LOOKING TO THE FUTURE while not losing sight of the vanishing landscapes our forefathers fashioned. PLEDGING to support RENEWABLE RESOURCES. Letting the reward of our work provide our needs while BRINGING JOY AND PEACE as it was intended.

Review Text: Golden Lighting is a manufacturer of residential light fixtures serving retailers across North America. To support internal analytics, the company partnered with Cuttlesoft to develop a web crawler that could gather data from various external websites and compile it into a standardized format. The goal was to streamline data collection and make it easier for internal teams to derive insights from structured information. Cuttlesoft delivered a simple yet effective solution by creating a script that automated the crawling process and organized the extracted data in a usable format. This eliminated the need for manual data gathering and significantly improved efficiency for the team. The project itself was relatively straightforward and was managed through a few phone calls and email communications, demonstrating the team’s ability to execute efficiently without heavy overhead. One of the most notable aspects of the engagement was the speed and simplicity of the solution. Despite minimal communication touchpoints, Cuttlesoft clearly understood the requirements and delivered a successful pilot, which led to further expansion of their role. While there was some initial misalignment regarding billing terms, it did not impact the overall success of the project. The solution met expectations and provided immediate value to the business. Overall, Cuttlesoft proved to be a reliable partner, delivering a practical and efficient solution that improved data collection processes and supported better decision-making within the organization.

Review Text: CrossComm partnered with Reveal Mobile to develop a specialized SDK designed to integrate seamlessly with both Android and iOS applications while supporting Bluetooth beacon detection. The primary objective was to enable large-scale, reliable data collection from mobile devices, forming the foundation for Reveal Mobile’s audience measurement and analytics platform. Since the internal team focused mainly on backend data processing and analytics, CrossComm contributed deep expertise in mobile SDK development and cutting-edge beacon technology. The SDK became a critical component of the platform, successfully collecting data from over one million unique devices daily and generating hundreds of millions of location events each month. This enabled Reveal Mobile to deliver precise audience insights and attribution capabilities to advertisers. Throughout the engagement, CrossComm maintained consistent and transparent communication, participating in weekly video calls, integrating with internal collaboration tools, and working closely with the engineering team to ensure seamless data flow between mobile and backend systems. They also helped refine development processes, improving coordination between outsourced and in-house teams. Their responsiveness, technical strength, and collaborative approach made them a reliable partner. Overall, the project significantly strengthened Reveal Mobile’s data infrastructure, enabling scalable and accurate data collection while supporting the company’s evolution into a leading measurement platform in the advertising ecosystem.

linkedin company about:
Pack Up + Go is a modern travel agency, planning trips around the United States. The catch? Your destination is a surprise. Until the day you depart.

Pack Up + Go revolutionizes domestic travel, using the element of surprise to heighten how American travelers perceive and experience their country. We take the pain out of planning by booking travel and accommodation, while providing curated city guides for excursions. Our customer seeks to explore the United States with an angle of spontaneity, ease, and adventure.  

We work to distribute the socioeconomic benefits of tourism among small and mid-sized cities, while encouraging travelers to experience places they may not seek out organically. Much of current American travel pivots on a few major metropolitan destinations, homogenizing the domestic travel experience. We challenge the status quo to make travel exciting and unique.

Customers select their budget (for travel + accommodation), and complete a quick survey detailing travel history and preferences. Given their budget and survey responses, our team selects the perfect destination for the trip. We send customers on their way with a city guide toolkit in hand - since travelers don't have time to do research on their destination, we do it for them! Pack Up + Go provides a curated map of recommendations, tailored to travelers'​ interests and preferences, all while supporting local businesses. We want travelers to feel as though their oldest friend is showing them around somewhere new.

There’s somewhere to explore this weekend. We do the work - you Pack Up + Go.

Review Text: Brad Sturgis, the CEO of Loudcloud Inc., engaged OpenForge starting in January 2016 for an ongoing project to rebuild and modernize their mobile calling and texting platform from the ground up. The primary objective was to transition the application to a hybrid Ionic framework, allowing for efficient code reusability across web and mobile platforms while completely overhauling the UX/UI design to improve the user interface. Working with a dedicated team of three, Brad has invested between $150,000 and $200,000 to date, resulting in a high-quality product that delivers all desired functionalities with exceptional stability. Brad noted that OpenForge’s transparent communication regarding time estimates and budget planning has been vital for their small telecommunications firm, and he was particularly impressed by the creative spirit the team brought to the design phase. The collaboration has proven to be a long-term success, with the app continuing to serve users worldwide through a maintainable and high-performing codebase that meets the specific demands of the telecommunications industry.
